Umehara's archaeological research spanned Japan, Korea, and China, where he contributed significantly to the establishment of typological methods in Japanese archaeology.
His distinctive personality led to conflicts with many contemporary archaeologists, including {{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yukio Kobayashi|ja|小林行雄|12=draft}}.
Umehara's son, {{ill|Kaoru Umehara|ja|梅原郁|12=draft}}, also became a distinguished archaeologist and professor emeritus at Kyoto University, specializing in East Asian history and winning the Japan Academy Prize in 2010.<ref>{{Cite journal|title=梅原郁先生略歴・主要業績目録 |url=http://id.nii.ac.jp/1642/00000645/ |journal=就実大学史学論集 |volume=36 |pages=9-22 |publisher=[[Shujitsu University]]|date=2022-03}}</ref>
== Awards ==
* 1962: {{ill|Asahi Prize|ja|朝日賞|12=draft}}<ref name=":0" /> * 1963: {{ill|Person of Cultural Merit|ja|文化功労者|12=draft}}<ref name=":0" /> * Order of the Rising Sun, Gold and Silver Star (勲二等旭日重光章)
== Selected Works ==
=== Books ===
* ''Study on Yayoi Pottery Styles'' (1919) * ''Research on Bronze Bells'' (1927) * ''Research on Chinese Ancient Mirrors'' (1931) * ''Collected Research on Chinese Archaeology'' (1938) * ''The Culture of Ancient Korea'' (1946) * ''Archaeological Observations on Ancient East Asia'' (1947) * ''Archaeology over Sixty Years'' (1973)
=== Co-authored and Edited ===
* ''Comprehensive Overview of Ancient Korean Culture'' (1947–1966) * ''The Tombs of Ancient Korea'' (1973)
== References ==
<references/>
== External Links ==
* [http://124.33.215.236/umehara2008/ume_query.html Umehara Archaeological Materials Database (Toyo Bunko)] * [http://124.33.215.236/umeharajpnjomon/umejpjmn_open_srchinput.php Japanese Jomon Period Archaeological Materials (Toyo Bunko)]

revision.comment: Bot: single‑shot reformat + ill→|12=draft + draft categories + iws + DEFAULTSORT + move HTML comments + meta‑cat check
revision.text:
'''Sueji Umehara''' (梅原 末治, August 13, 1893 – February 19, 1983)<ref name=":0">{{Citation|title=本会顧問 梅原末治博士訃|author=有光教一|year=1983-07-01|url=https://doi.org/10.14989/shirin_66_591|publisher=史学研究会 (京都大学文学部内)|language=ja|doi=10.14989/shirin_66_591|access-date=2022-08-22}}</ref> was a Japanese {{ill|archaeologist|ja|考古学者|12=draft|12=draft}} specializing in East Asian archaeology, particularly in {{ill|Chinese archaeology|ja|中国考古学|12=draft|12=draft}}. He served as a professor emeritus at {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name=":0" /> and was instrumental in establishing the foundations of East Asian archaeology.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=梅原末治 |url=http://www.toyo-bunko.or.jp/library3/shozou/umehara.html |website=www.toyo-bunko.or.jp |access-date=2022-08-22 |publisher=公益財団法人[[東洋文庫]]}}</ref> He earned his Doctor of Letters (文学博士) from Kyoto Imperial University in 1939 for his dissertation, ''A Study of the Bronze Age in China'' (支那青銅器時代の研究).
== Biography ==
Umehara was born in {{ill|Furuichi Village|ja|古市町 (大阪府)|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture (now {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft|12=draft}}).<ref name=":1" /> He graduated from Dōshisha Junior High School (now {{ill|Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|ja|同志社中学校・高等学校|12=draft|12=draft}}) in 1913.<ref name="rireki">[https://kensaku.kua1.archives.kyoto-u.ac.jp/rireki/?c=detail&id=000149 詳細情報一覧/歴代総長・教授・助教授履歴検索システム－対象：1949th year 以前の在職者―]</ref> In 1914, he became an assistant at the Department of Archaeology at Kyoto Imperial University. In 1921, he was a11ointed as a member of the Archaeological Survey Committee of the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府|12=draft|12=draft}}. He joined the {{ill|Kyoto Research Institute|ja|東方文化学院京都研究所|12=draft|12=draft}} (now the {{ill|Institute for Research in Humanities, Kyoto University|ja|京都大学人文科学研究所|12=draft|12=draft}}) in 1929 and became a lecturer at Kyoto Imperial University.<ref name=":0" /> In June 1933, he was promoted to associate professor in the Department of Archaeology.<ref name="rireki" /> In 1939, he obtained his doctorate and was a11ointed professor.<ref name="rireki" />
After his retirement in 1956, Umehara was named professor emeritus at Kyoto University.<ref name="rireki" />
== Contributions ==
Despite being frail and unable to pursue higher education initially, Umehara studied under {{ill|Kosaku Hamada|ja|濱田耕作|12=draft|12=draft}}, the founder of the archaeology department at Kyoto University. Umehara's archaeological research spanned Japan, Korea, and China, where he contributed significantly to the establishment of typological methods in Japanese archaeology.
His distinctive personality led to conflicts with many contemporary archaeologists, including {{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yukio Kobayashi|ja|小林行雄|12=draft|12=draft}}.
Umehara's son, {{ill|Kaoru Umehara|ja|梅原郁|12=draft|12=draft}}, also became a distinguished archaeologist and professor emeritus at Kyoto University, specializing in East Asian history and winning the Japan Academy Prize in 2010.<ref>{{Cite journal|title=梅原郁先生略歴・主要業績目録 |url=http://id.nii.ac.jp/1642/00000645/ |journal=就実大学史学論集 |volume=36 |pages=9-22 |publisher=[[Shujitsu University]]|date=2022-03}}</ref>
== Awards ==
* 1962: {{ill|Asahi Prize|ja|朝日賞|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name=":0" /> * 1963: {{ill|Person of Cultural Merit|ja|文化功労者|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name=":0" /> * Order of the Rising Sun, Gold and Silver Star (勲二等旭日重光章)
== Selected Works ==
=== Books ===
* ''Study on Yayoi Pottery Styles'' (1919) * ''Research on Bronze Bells'' (1927) * ''Research on Chinese Ancient Mirrors'' (1931) * ''Collected Research on Chinese Archaeology'' (1938) * ''The Culture of Ancient Korea'' (1946) * ''Archaeological Observations on Ancient East Asia'' (1947) * ''Archaeology over Sixty Years'' (1973)
=== Co-authored and Edited ===
* ''Comprehensive Overview of Ancient Korean Culture'' (1947–1966) * ''The Tombs of Ancient Korea'' (1973)
== References ==
<references/>
== External Links ==
* [http://124.33.215.236/umehara2008/ume_query.html Umehara Archaeological Materials Database (Toyo Bunko)] * [http://124.33.215.236/umeharajpnjomon/umejpjmn_open_srchinput.php Japanese Jomon Period Archaeological Materials (Toyo Bunko)]

revision.comment: 76 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:梅原末治 up to rev 103135682
revision.text:
{{出典の明記| date = 2020年9月}} {{Infobox 学者 |name = {{ruby|梅原 末治|うめはら すえじ}} |image =Sueji Umehara 1925.jpg |image_size = |caption =1925年 |birth_date = {{birth date|1893|8|13}} |birth_place = {{JPN}}[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[古市町 (大阪府)|古市村]] |death_date = {{death date and age|1983|2|19|1893|8|13}} |death_place = |othername = |main_interests = 東洋考古学、[[中国考古学]] |alma_mater = <!--母校--> |workplaces = [[京都大学]] |degree = [[博士（文学）|文学博士]]（京都帝国大学、1939年）（[[学位論文]]「支那青銅器時代の研究」） |URL = |yearsactive = |spouse = |children = }} '''梅原 末治'''（うめはら すえじ、[[1893年]][[8月13日]] - [[1983年]][[2月19日]]<ref name=":0">{{Citation|和書|title=本会顧問 梅原末治博士訃|author=有光教一|year=1983-07-01|url=https://doi.org/10.14989/shirin_66_591|publisher=史学研究会 (京都大学文学部内)|language=ja|doi=10.14989/shirin_66_591|access-date=2022-08-22}}</ref>）は、日本の[[東洋]][[考古学者]]。[[京都大学]][[名誉教授]]<ref name=":0" />。東洋考古学の基礎を築いた<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|和書|title=梅原末治 |url=http://www.toyo-bunko.or.jp/library3/shozou/umehara.html |website=www.toyo-bunko.or.jp |access-date=2022-08-22 |publisher=公益財団法人[[東洋文庫]]}}</ref>。[[博士（文学）|文学博士]]（[[京都大学#沿革|京都帝国大学]]・1939年）（[[学位論文]]「支那青銅器時代の研究」）。
== 略歴 == [[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[古市町 (大阪府)|古市村]]（現：[[羽曳野市]]）に生まれる<ref name=":1" />。1913年同志社普通学校（現：[[同志社中学校・高等学校]]）卒業<ref name="rireki" >[https://kensaku.kua1.archives.kyoto-u.ac.jp/rireki/?c=detail&id=000149 詳細情報一覧/歴代総長・教授・助教授履歴検索システム－対象：1949年以前の在職者―]</ref>。1914年[[京都大学#沿革|京都帝国大学]]文科大学陳列館[[助手 (教育)|助手]]。1921年[[朝鮮総督府]]古跡調査委員{{要出典|date=2022-08}}。1929年、[[東方文化学院]]京都研究所（現：[[京都大学人文科学研究所]]）所員<ref name=":0" />、京都帝国大学文学部講師<ref name=":0" />。1933年6月、同[[助教授]]（考古学講座）<ref name="rireki" />。1939年「支那青銅器時代の研究」で京都大学より[[博士（文学）|文学博士]]の学位を取得<ref name="rireki" />、同[[教授]]（考古学講座）<ref name="rireki" />。
1948年7月、京都大学[[評議員]]（- 同年11月）<ref name="rireki" />、1956年8月、京都大学[[名誉教授]]<ref name="rireki" />。
== 人物 == {{出典の明記|section=1|date=2022-08}} 病弱のため大学進学は叶わなかった。[[日本]]で初めて京大に考古学教室を開いたことで知られる[[濱田耕作]]に師事し、多年にわたり日本ばかりでなく朝鮮、中国にて考古学の研究に従事し、功績を残した。型式学的研究法の確立で日本考古学に貢献した。
個性的な性格のため、多くの同年代の考古学者（[[末永雅雄]]、[[小林行雄]]など多数）との確執を生んだ。<!--晩年には骨董商から偽物をつかまされたり、京都大学の退官記念論文集が出版されない（誰も退官記念論文集の発行を提案しなかったと言われている）などといったことがあった。-->
息子の[[梅原郁]]も考古学者で、京都大学名誉教授（人文科学研究所、[[東洋史]]、宋代官僚制・司法制度研究で、2010年[[日本学士院賞]]受賞）<ref>{{Cite journal|和書 |author= |date=2022-03 |title=梅原郁先生略歴・主要業績目録 |url=http://id.nii.ac.jp/1642/00000645/ |journal=就実大学史学論集 |volume=36 |pages=9-22 |publisher=[[就実大学]]人文科学部総合歴史学科|ref=harv}}</ref>。 *『東方学回想 Ⅵ 学問の思い出〈2〉』[[東方学会]]編、[[刀水書房]]、2000年。座談会での回想が収録 *『考古学京都学派』[[角田文衛]]編、雄山閣出版、増補1997年。第3部に梅原末治論
== 受賞歴 == *[[1963年]] - 1962年度[[朝日賞]]<ref name=":0" /> *[[1963年]] - [[文化功労者]]<ref name=":0" /> *[[従三位]][[勲二等]][[旭日重光章]]
== 著書 == *「弥生土器形式分類聚成図録」（『京都帝国大学文学部考古学研究報告3』、1919年度）最初の図録を作成。 *『鑑鏡の研究』大岡山書店 1925 *『[[銅鐸]]の研究』大岡山書店 1927。木耳社 1985 *『欧米に於ける支那古鏡』刀江書院 1931 *『漢三国六朝紀年鏡集録』岡書院 1931 *『古代北方系文物の研究 明斯教授頌寿記念』[[星野書店]] 1938。新時代社 1971 *『支那考古学論攷』[[弘文堂]] 1938 *『紹興古鏡聚英』桑名文星堂 1939。同朋舎出版 1984 *『日本考古学論攷』弘文堂書房、1940 *『河南安陽遺物の研究』桑名文星堂 1941 同朋舎出版 1984 *『漢三国六朝紀年鏡図説』桑名文星堂 1943 京都帝国大学文学部考古学資料叢刊・同朋舎出版 1984 *『支那漢代紀年銘漆器図説』桑名文星堂 1943 京都帝国大学文学部考古学資料叢刊・同朋舎出版 1984 *『東亜考古学論攷』第1 星野書店 1944 *『唐鏡大観』美術書院 1945 京都帝国大学文学部考古学資料叢刊・同朋舎出版 1984 *『朝鮮古代の文化』高桐書院 1946。国書刊行会 1972 *『東亜の古代文化』養徳社 1946 *『朝鮮古代の墓制』座右宝刊行会 1947。[[国書刊行会]] 1972 *『東亜考古学概観』[[星野書店]] 1947 *『日本の古墳墓』養徳社 1947 *『支那古玉図録』桑名文星堂 1955 京都帝国大学文学部考古学資料叢刊・同朋舎出版 1984 *『殷墓発見木器印影図録』[[便利堂]] 1959 *『日本蒐儲支那古銅精華』第1-6編 山中商会 1959-1964 *『[[殷墟]]』[[朝日新聞社]] 1965 *『日本古玉器雑攷』[[吉川弘文館]]、1971 *『考古学六十年』[[平凡社]] 1973 *『殷墟出土白色土器の研究』[[同朋舎出版]] 1984 *『河南安陽遺宝』同朋舎出版 1984 *『漢以前の古鏡の研究』同朋舎出版 1984 *『古銅器形態の考古学的研究』同朋舎出版 1984 *『戦国式銅器の研究』同朋舎出版 1984 *『柉禁の考古学的考察』同朋舎出版 1984 *『洛陽金村古墓聚英』同朋舎出版 1984
=== 共編著 === *『朝鮮古文化綜鑑』第1-4巻 [[藤田亮策]]共編著 養徳社 1947-1966 *『通溝 満州国通化省輯安県高句麗遺蹟』[[池内宏]]共著 [[国書刊行会]] 1973、2冊組 *編『慶州金鈴塚飾履塚 大正13年度古蹟調査報告』図書刊行会 1973、復刻版
== 脚注 == <references/>
== 外部リンク == * [http://124.33.215.236/umehara2008/ume_query.html 公益財団法人東洋文庫 画像データベース 梅原考古資料] * [http://124.33.215.236/umeharajpnjomon/umejpjmn_open_srchinput.php 公益財団法人東洋文庫 画像データベース 梅原考古資料日本縄文時代之部]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[梅原末治]] to [[Sueji Umehara]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{出典の明記| date = 2020年9月}} {{Infobox 学者 |name = {{ruby|梅原 末治|うめはら すえじ}} |image =Sueji Umehara 1925.jpg |image_size = |caption =1925年 |birth_date = {{birth date|1893|8|13}} |birth_place = {{JPN}}[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[古市町 (大阪府)|古市村]] |death_date = {{death date and age|1983|2|19|1893|8|13}} |death_place = |othername = |main_interests = 東洋考古学、[[中国考古学]] |alma_mater = <!--母校--> |workplaces = [[京都大学]] |degree = [[博士（文学）|文学博士]]（京都帝国大学、1939年）（[[学位論文]]「支那青銅器時代の研究」） |URL = |yearsactive = |spouse = |children = }} '''梅原 末治'''（うめはら すえじ、[[1893年]][[8月13日]] - [[1983年]][[2月19日]]<ref name=":0">{{Citation|和書|title=本会顧問 梅原末治博士訃|author=有光教一|year=1983-07-01|url=https://doi.org/10.14989/shirin_66_591|publisher=史学研究会 (京都大学文学部内)|language=ja|doi=10.14989/shirin_66_591|access-date=2022-08-22}}</ref>）は、日本の[[東洋]][[考古学者]]。[[京都大学]][[名誉教授]]<ref name=":0" />。東洋考古学の基礎を築いた<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|和書|title=梅原末治 |url=http://www.toyo-bunko.or.jp/library3/shozou/umehara.html |website=www.toyo-bunko.or.jp |access-date=2022-08-22 |publisher=公益財団法人[[東洋文庫]]}}</ref>。[[博士（文学）|文学博士]]（[[京都大学#沿革|京都帝国大学]]・1939年）（[[学位論文]]「支那青銅器時代の研究」）。
== 略歴 == [[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[古市町 (大阪府)|古市村]]（現：[[羽曳野市]]）に生まれる<ref name=":1" />。1913年同志社普通学校（現：[[同志社中学校・高等学校]]）卒業<ref name="rireki" >[https://kensaku.kua1.archives.kyoto-u.ac.jp/rireki/?c=detail&id=000149 詳細情報一覧/歴代総長・教授・助教授履歴検索システム－対象：1949年以前の在職者―]</ref>。1914年[[京都大学#沿革|京都帝国大学]]文科大学陳列館[[助手 (教育)|助手]]。1921年[[朝鮮総督府]]古跡調査委員{{要出典|date=2022-08}}。1929年、[[東方文化学院]]京都研究所（現：[[京都大学人文科学研究所]]）所員<ref name=":0" />、京都帝国大学文学部講師<ref name=":0" />。1933年6月、同[[助教授]]（考古学講座）<ref name="rireki" />。1939年「支那青銅器時代の研究」で京都大学より[[博士（文学）|文学博士]]の学位を取得<ref name="rireki" />、同[[教授]]（考古学講座）<ref name="rireki" />。
1948年7月、京都大学[[評議員]]（- 同年11月）<ref name="rireki" />、1956年8月、京都大学[[名誉教授]]<ref name="rireki" />。
== 人物 == {{出典の明記|section=1|date=2022-08}} 病弱のため大学進学は叶わなかった。[[日本]]で初めて京大に考古学教室を開いたことで知られる[[濱田耕作]]に師事し、多年にわたり日本ばかりでなく朝鮮、中国にて考古学の研究に従事し、功績を残した。型式学的研究法の確立で日本考古学に貢献した。
個性的な性格のため、多くの同年代の考古学者（[[末永雅雄]]、[[小林行雄]]など多数）との確執を生んだ。<!--晩年には骨董商から偽物をつかまされたり、京都大学の退官記念論文集が出版されない（誰も退官記念論文集の発行を提案しなかったと言われている）などといったことがあった。-->
息子の[[梅原郁]]も考古学者で、京都大学名誉教授（人文科学研究所、[[東洋史]]、宋代官僚制・司法制度研究で、2010年[[日本学士院賞]]受賞）<ref>{{Cite journal|和書 |author= |date=2022-03 |title=梅原郁先生略歴・主要業績目録 |url=http://id.nii.ac.jp/1642/00000645/ |journal=就実大学史学論集 |volume=36 |pages=9-22 |publisher=[[就実大学]]人文科学部総合歴史学科|ref=harv}}</ref>。 *『東方学回想 Ⅵ 学問の思い出〈2〉』[[東方学会]]編、[[刀水書房]]、2000年。座談会での回想が収録 *『考古学京都学派』[[角田文衛]]編、雄山閣出版、増補1997年。第3部に梅原末治論
== 受賞歴 == *[[1963年]] - 1962年度[[朝日賞]]<ref name=":0" /> *[[1963年]] - [[文化功労者]]<ref name=":0" /> *[[従三位]][[勲二等]][[旭日重光章]]
== 著書 == *「弥生土器形式分類聚成図録」（『京都帝国大学文学部考古学研究報告3』、1919年度）最初の図録を作成。 *『鑑鏡の研究』大岡山書店 1925 *『[[銅鐸]]の研究』大岡山書店 1927。木耳社 1985 *『欧米に於ける支那古鏡』刀江書院 1931 *『漢三国六朝紀年鏡集録』岡書院 1931 *『古代北方系文物の研究 明斯教授頌寿記念』[[星野書店]] 1938。新時代社 1971 *『支那考古学論攷』[[弘文堂]] 1938 *『紹興古鏡聚英』桑名文星堂 1939。同朋舎出版 1984 *『日本考古学論攷』弘文堂書房、1940 *『河南安陽遺物の研究』桑名文星堂 1941 同朋舎出版 1984 *『漢三国六朝紀年鏡図説』桑名文星堂 1943 京都帝国大学文学部考古学資料叢刊・同朋舎出版 1984 *『支那漢代紀年銘漆器図説』桑名文星堂 1943 京都帝国大学文学部考古学資料叢刊・同朋舎出版 1984 *『東亜考古学論攷』第1 星野書店 1944 *『唐鏡大観』美術書院 1945 京都帝国大学文学部考古学資料叢刊・同朋舎出版 1984 *『朝鮮古代の文化』高桐書院 1946。国書刊行会 1972 *『東亜の古代文化』養徳社 1946 *『朝鮮古代の墓制』座右宝刊行会 1947。[[国書刊行会]] 1972 *『東亜考古学概観』[[星野書店]] 1947 *『日本の古墳墓』養徳社 1947 *『支那古玉図録』桑名文星堂 1955 京都帝国大学文学部考古学資料叢刊・同朋舎出版 1984 *『殷墓発見木器印影図録』[[便利堂]] 1959 *『日本蒐儲支那古銅精華』第1-6編 山中商会 1959-1964 *『[[殷墟]]』[[朝日新聞社]] 1965 *『日本古玉器雑攷』[[吉川弘文館]]、1971 *『考古学六十年』[[平凡社]] 1973 *『殷墟出土白色土器の研究』[[同朋舎出版]] 1984 *『河南安陽遺宝』同朋舎出版 1984 *『漢以前の古鏡の研究』同朋舎出版 1984 *『古銅器形態の考古学的研究』同朋舎出版 1984 *『戦国式銅器の研究』同朋舎出版 1984 *『柉禁の考古学的考察』同朋舎出版 1984 *『洛陽金村古墓聚英』同朋舎出版 1984
=== 共編著 === *『朝鮮古文化綜鑑』第1-4巻 [[藤田亮策]]共編著 養徳社 1947-1966 *『通溝 満州国通化省輯安県高句麗遺蹟』[[池内宏]]共著 [[国書刊行会]] 1973、2冊組 *編『慶州金鈴塚飾履塚 大正13年度古蹟調査報告』図書刊行会 1973、復刻版
== 脚注 == <references/>
== 外部リンク == * [http://124.33.215.236/umehara2008/ume_query.html 公益財団法人東洋文庫 画像データベース 梅原考古資料] * [http://124.33.215.236/umeharajpnjomon/umejpjmn_open_srchinput.php 公益財団法人東洋文庫 画像データベース 梅原考古資料日本縄文時代之部]
{{Academic-bio-stub}} {{Normdaten}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:うめはら すえし}} [[Category:日本の考古学者]] [[Category:京都大学の教員]] [[Category:京都大学人文科学研究所の人物]] [[Category:奈良国立博物館の人物]] [[Category:文化功労者]] [[Category:勲二等旭日重光章受章者]] [[Category:同志社高等学校出身の人物]] [[Category:大阪府出身の人物]] [[Category:1893年生]] [[Category:1983年没]]

'''Sueji Umehara''' (梅原 末治, August 13, 1893 – February 19, 1983)<ref name=":0">{{Citation|title=本会顧問 梅原末治博士訃|author=有光教一|year=1983-07-01|url=https://doi.org/10.14989/shirin_66_591|publisher=史学研究会 (京都大学文学部内)|language=ja|doi=10.14989/shirin_66_591|access-date=2022-08-22}}</ref> was a Japanese {{ill|archaeologist|ja|考古学者|qq=draft|qq=draft}} specializing in East Asian archaeology, particularly in {{ill|Chinese archaeology|ja|中国考古学|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. He served as a professor emeritus at {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name=":0" /> and was instrumental in establishing the foundations of East Asian archaeology.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=梅原末治 |url=http://www.toyo-bunko.or.jp/library3/shozou/umehara.html |website=www.toyo-bunko.or.jp |access-date=2022-08-22 |publisher=公益財団法人[[東洋文庫]]}}</ref> He earned his Doctor of Letters (文学博士) from Kyoto Imperial University in 1939 for his dissertation, ''A Study of the Bronze Age in China'' (支那青銅器時代の研究).
== Biography ==
Umehara was born in {{ill|Furuichi Village|ja|古市町 (大阪府)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture (now {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}).<ref name=":1" /> He graduated from Dōshisha Junior High School (now {{ill|Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|ja|同志社中学校・高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) in 1913.<ref name="rireki">[https://kensaku.kua1.archives.kyoto-u.ac.jp/rireki/?c=detail&id=000149 詳細情報一覧/歴代総長・教授・助教授履歴検索システム－対象：1949th year 以前の在職者―]</ref> In 1914, he became an assistant at the Department of Archaeology at Kyoto Imperial University. In 1921, he was a11ointed as a member of the Archaeological Survey Committee of the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. He joined the {{ill|Kyoto Research Institute|ja|東方文化学院京都研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (now the {{ill|Institute for Research in Humanities, Kyoto University|ja|京都大学人文科学研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) in 1929 and became a lecturer at Kyoto Imperial University.<ref name=":0" /> In June 1933, he was promoted to associate professor in the Department of Archaeology.<ref name="rireki" /> In 1939, he obtained his doctorate and was a11ointed professor.<ref name="rireki" />
After his retirement in 1956, Umehara was named professor emeritus at Kyoto University.<ref name="rireki" />
== Contributions ==
Despite being frail and unable to pursue higher education initially, Umehara studied under {{ill|Kosaku Hamada|ja|濱田耕作|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the founder of the archaeology department at Kyoto University. Umehara's archaeological research spanned Japan, Korea, and China, where he contributed significantly to the establishment of typological methods in Japanese archaeology.
His distinctive personality led to conflicts with many contemporary archaeologists, including {{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Yukio Kobayashi|ja|小林行雄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
Umehara's son, {{ill|Kaoru Umehara|ja|梅原郁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, also became a distinguished archaeologist and professor emeritus at Kyoto University, specializing in East Asian history and winning the Japan Academy Prize in 2010.<ref>{{Cite journal|title=梅原郁先生略歴・主要業績目録 |url=http://id.nii.ac.jp/1642/00000645/ |journal=就実大学史学論集 |volume=36 |pages=9-22 |publisher=[[Shujitsu University]]|date=2022-03}}</ref>
== Awards ==
* 1962: {{ill|Asahi Prize|ja|朝日賞|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name=":0" /> * 1963: {{ill|Person of Cultural Merit|ja|文化功労者|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name=":0" /> * Order of the Rising Sun, Gold and Silver Star (勲二等旭日重光章)
== Selected Works ==
=== Books ===
* ''Study on Yayoi Pottery Styles'' (1919) * ''Research on Bronze Bells'' (1927) * ''Research on Chinese Ancient Mirrors'' (1931) * ''Collected Research on Chinese Archaeology'' (1938) * ''The Culture of Ancient Korea'' (1946) * ''Archaeological Observations on Ancient East Asia'' (1947) * ''Archaeology over Sixty Years'' (1973)
=== Co-authored and Edited ===
* ''Comprehensive Overview of Ancient Korean Culture'' (1947–1966) * ''The Tombs of Ancient Korea'' (1973)
== References ==
<references/>
== External Links ==
* [http://124.33.215.236/umehara2008/ume_query.html Umehara Archaeological Materials Database (Toyo Bunko)] * [http://124.33.215.236/umeharajpnjomon/umejpjmn_open_srchinput.php Japanese Jomon Period Archaeological Materials (Toyo Bunko)]

In 1921, he was a11ointed as a member of the Archaeological Survey Committee of the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. He joined the {{ill|Kyoto Research Institute|ja|東方文化学院京都研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (now the {{ill|Institute for Research in Humanities, Kyoto University|ja|京都大学人文科学研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) in 1929 and became a lecturer at Kyoto Imperial University.<ref name=":0" /> In June 1933, he was promoted to associate professor in the Department of Archaeology.<ref name="rireki" /> In 1939, he obtained his doctorate and was a11ointed professor.<ref name="rireki" />
After his retirement in 1956, Umehara was named professor emeritus at Kyoto University.<ref name="rireki" />
== Contributions ==
Despite being frail and unable to pursue higher education initially, Umehara studied under {{ill|Kosaku Hamada|ja|濱田耕作|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the founder of the archaeology department at Kyoto University. Umehara's archaeological research spanned Japan, Korea, and China, where he contributed significantly to the establishment of typological methods in Japanese archaeology.
His distinctive personality led to conflicts with many contemporary archaeologists, including {{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Yukio Kobayashi|ja|小林行雄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
Umehara's son, {{ill|Kaoru Umehara|ja|梅原郁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, also became a distinguished archaeologist and professor emeritus at Kyoto University, specializing in East Asian history and winning the Japan Academy Prize in 2010.<ref>{{Cite journal|title=梅原郁先生略歴・主要業績目録 |url=http://id.nii.ac.jp/1642/00000645/ |journal=就実大学史学論集 |volume=36 |pages=9-22 |publisher=[[Shujitsu University]]|date=2022-03}}</ref>

His distinctive personality led to conflicts with many contemporary archaeologists, including {{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masao Suenaga|qq=}} and {{ill|Yukio Kobayashi|ja|小林行雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukio Kobayashi|qq=}}.
Umehara's son, {{ill|Kaoru Umehara|ja|梅原郁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaoru Umehara|qq=}}, also became a distinguished archaeologist and professor emeritus at Kyoto University, specializing in East Asian history and winning the Japan Academy Prize in 2010.<ref>{{Cite journal|title=梅原郁先生略歴・主要業績目録 |url=http://id.nii.ac.jp/1642/00000645/ |journal=就実大学史学論集 |volume=36 |pages=9-22 |publisher=[[Shujitsu University]]|date=2022-03}}</ref>
== Awards ==
* 1962: {{ill|Asahi Prize|ja|朝日賞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Prize|qq=}}<ref name=":0" /> * 1963: {{ill|Person of Cultural Merit|ja|文化功労者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Person of Cultural Merit|qq=}}<ref name=":0" /> * Order of the Rising Sun, Gold and Silver Star (勲二等旭日重光章)
== Selected Works ==
=== Books ===
* ''Study on Yayoi Pottery Styles'' (1919) * ''Research on Bronze Bells'' (1927) * ''Research on Chinese Ancient Mirrors'' (1931) * ''Collected Research on Chinese Archaeology'' (1938) * ''The Culture of Ancient Korea'' (1946) * ''Archaeological Observations on Ancient East Asia'' (1947) * ''Archaeology over Sixty Years'' (1973)

revision.comment: Bot: remove sole member from [[Category:People from Osaka Prefecture]]
revision.text:
'''Sueji Umehara''' (梅原 末治, August 13, 1893 – February 19, 1983)<ref name=":0">{{Citation|title=本会顧問 梅原末治博士訃|author=有光教一|year=1983-07-01|url=https://doi.org/10.14989/shirin_66_591|publisher=史学研究会 (京都大学文学部内)|language=ja|doi=10.14989/shirin_66_591|access-date=2022-08-22}}</ref> was a Japanese {{ill|archaeologist|ja|考古学者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/archaeologist|qq=}} specializing in East Asian archaeology, particularly in {{ill|Chinese archaeology|ja|中国考古学|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese archaeology|qq=}}. He served as a professor emeritus at {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto University|qq=}}<ref name=":0" /> and was instrumental in establishing the foundations of East Asian archaeology.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=梅原末治 |url=http://www.toyo-bunko.or.jp/library3/shozou/umehara.html |website=www.toyo-bunko.or.jp |access-date=2022-08-22 |publisher=公益財団法人[[東洋文庫]]}}</ref> He earned his Doctor of Letters (文学博士) from Kyoto Imperial University in 1939 for his dissertation, ''A Study of the Bronze Age in China'' (支那青銅器時代の研究).
== Biography ==
Umehara was born in {{ill|Furuichi Village|ja|古市町 (大阪府)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Village|qq=}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamikawachi District, Osaka|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture (now {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino|qq=}}).<ref name=":1" /> He graduated from Dōshisha Junior High School (now {{ill|Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|ja|同志社中学校・高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|qq=}}) in 1913.<ref name="rireki">[https://kensaku.kua1.archives.kyoto-u.ac.jp/rireki/?c=detail&id=000149 詳細情報一覧/歴代総長・教授・助教授履歴検索システム－対象：1949th year 以前の在職者―]</ref> In 1914, he became an assistant at the Department of Archaeology at Kyoto Imperial University. In 1921, he was a11ointed as a member of the Archaeological Survey Committee of the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government-General of Korea|qq=}}. He joined the {{ill|Kyoto Research Institute|ja|東方文化学院京都研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Research Institute|qq=}} (now the {{ill|Institute for Research in Humanities, Kyoto University|ja|京都大学人文科学研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Institute for Research in Humanities, Kyoto University|qq=}}) in 1929 and became a lecturer at Kyoto Imperial University.<ref name=":0" /> In June 1933, he was promoted to associate professor in the Department of Archaeology.<ref name="rireki" /> In 1939, he obtained his doctorate and was a11ointed professor.<ref name="rireki" />
After his retirement in 1956, Umehara was named professor emeritus at Kyoto University.<ref name="rireki" />
== Contributions ==
Despite being frail and unable to pursue higher education initially, Umehara studied under {{ill|Kosaku Hamada|ja|濱田耕作|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosaku Hamada|qq=}}, the founder of the archaeology department at Kyoto University. Umehara's archaeological research spanned Japan, Korea, and China, where he contributed significantly to the establishment of typological methods in Japanese archaeology.
His distinctive personality led to conflicts with many contemporary archaeologists, including {{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masao Suenaga|qq=}} and {{ill|Yukio Kobayashi|ja|小林行雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukio Kobayashi|qq=}}.
Umehara's son, {{ill|Kaoru Umehara|ja|梅原郁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaoru Umehara|qq=}}, also became a distinguished archaeologist and professor emeritus at Kyoto University, specializing in East Asian history and winning the Japan Academy Prize in 2010.<ref>{{Cite journal|title=梅原郁先生略歴・主要業績目録 |url=http://id.nii.ac.jp/1642/00000645/ |journal=就実大学史学論集 |volume=36 |pages=9-22 |publisher=[[Shujitsu University]]|date=2022-03}}</ref>
== Awards ==
* 1962: {{ill|Asahi Prize|ja|朝日賞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Prize|qq=}}<ref name=":0" /> * 1963: {{ill|Person of Cultural Merit|ja|文化功労者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Person of Cultural Merit|qq=}}<ref name=":0" /> * Order of the Rising Sun, Gold and Silver Star (勲二等旭日重光章)
== Selected Works ==
=== Books ===
* ''Study on Yayoi Pottery Styles'' (1919) * ''Research on Bronze Bells'' (1927) * ''Research on Chinese Ancient Mirrors'' (1931) * ''Collected Research on Chinese Archaeology'' (1938) * ''The Culture of Ancient Korea'' (1946) * ''Archaeological Observations on Ancient East Asia'' (1947) * ''Archaeology over Sixty Years'' (1973)
=== Co-authored and Edited ===
* ''Comprehensive Overview of Ancient Korean Culture'' (1947–1966) * ''The Tombs of Ancient Korea'' (1973)

Umehara's archaeological research spanned Japan, Korea, and China, where he contributed significantly to the establishment of typological methods in Japanese archaeology.
His distinctive personality led to conflicts with many contemporary archaeologists, including {{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masao Suenaga|qq=}} and {{ill|Yukio Kobayashi|ja|小林行雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukio Kobayashi|qq=}}.
Umehara's son, {{ill|Kaoru Umehara|ja|梅原郁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaoru Umehara|qq=}}, also became a distinguished archaeologist and professor emeritus at Kyoto University, specializing in East Asian history and winning the Japan Academy Prize in 2010.<ref>{{Cite journal|title=梅原郁先生略歴・主要業績目録 |url=http://id.nii.ac.jp/1642/00000645/ |journal=就実大学史学論集 |volume=36 |pages=9-22 |publisher=[[Shujitsu University]]|date=2022-03}}</ref>

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:京都大学人文科学研究所の人物
revision.text:
'''Sueji Umehara''' (梅原 末治, August 13, 1893 – February 19, 1983)<ref name=":0">{{Citation|title=本会顧問 梅原末治博士訃|author=有光教一|year=1983-07-01|url=https://doi.org/10.14989/shirin_66_591|publisher=史学研究会 (京都大学文学部内)|language=ja|doi=10.14989/shirin_66_591|access-date=2022-08-22}}</ref> was a Japanese {{ill|archaeologist|ja|考古学者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/archaeologist|qq=}} specializing in East Asian archaeology, particularly in [[:en:Archaeology of China|Chinese archaeology]]. He served as a professor emeritus at [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]]<ref name=":0" /> and was instrumental in establishing the foundations of East Asian archaeology.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=梅原末治 |url=http://www.toyo-bunko.or.jp/library3/shozou/umehara.html |website=www.toyo-bunko.or.jp |access-date=2022-08-22 |publisher=公益財団法人[[東洋文庫]]}}</ref> He earned his Doctor of Letters (文学博士) from Kyoto Imperial University in 1939 for his dissertation, ''A Study of the Bronze Age in China'' (支那青銅器時代の研究).
== Biography ==
Umehara was born in {{ill|Furuichi Village|ja|古市町 (大阪府)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Village|qq=}}, [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture (now [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]]).<ref name=":1" /> He graduated from Dōshisha Junior High School (now {{ill|Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|ja|同志社中学校・高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|qq=}}) in 1913.<ref name="rireki">[https://kensaku.kua1.archives.kyoto-u.ac.jp/rireki/?c=detail&id=000149 詳細情報一覧/歴代総長・教授・助教授履歴検索システム－対象：1949th year 以前の在職者―]</ref> In 1914, he became an assistant at the Department of Archaeology at Kyoto Imperial University. In 1921, he was a11ointed as a member of the Archaeological Survey Committee of the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government-General of Korea|qq=}}. He joined the {{ill|Kyoto Research Institute|ja|東方文化学院京都研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Research Institute|qq=}} (now the [[:en:The Kyoto University Research Centre for the Cultural Sciences|Institute for Research in Humanities, Kyoto University]]) in 1929 and became a lecturer at Kyoto Imperial University.<ref name=":0" /> In June 1933, he was promoted to associate professor in the Department of Archaeology.<ref name="rireki" /> In 1939, he obtained his doctorate and was a11ointed professor.<ref name="rireki" />
After his retirement in 1956, Umehara was named professor emeritus at Kyoto University.<ref name="rireki" />
== Contributions ==
Despite being frail and unable to pursue higher education initially, Umehara studied under [[:en:Kōsaku Hamada|Kosaku Hamada]], the founder of the archaeology department at Kyoto University. His distinctive personality led to conflicts with many contemporary archaeologists, including {{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masao Suenaga|qq=}} and {{ill|Yukio Kobayashi|ja|小林行雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukio Kobayashi|qq=}}.
Umehara's son, {{ill|Kaoru Umehara|ja|梅原郁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaoru Umehara|qq=}}, also became a distinguished archaeologist and professor emeritus at Kyoto University, specializing in East Asian history and winning the Japan Academy Prize in 2010.<ref>{{Cite journal|title=梅原郁先生略歴・主要業績目録 |url=http://id.nii.ac.jp/1642/00000645/ |journal=就実大学史学論集 |volume=36 |pages=9-22 |publisher=[[Shujitsu University]]|date=2022-03}}</ref>

<!--Sueji Umehara--><!--Sueji Umehara--> [[Category:Academic staff of Kyoto University]] [[Category:京都大学人文科学研究所の人物]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Rising Sun, 2nd class]] [[Category:同志社高等学校出身の人物]] [[Category:大阪府出身の人物]]

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:奈良国立博物館の人物
revision.text:
'''Sueji Umehara''' (梅原 末治, August 13, 1893 – February 19, 1983)<ref name=":0">{{Citation|title=本会顧問 梅原末治博士訃|author=有光教一|year=1983-07-01|url=https://doi.org/10.14989/shirin_66_591|publisher=史学研究会 (京都大学文学部内)|language=ja|doi=10.14989/shirin_66_591|access-date=2022-08-22}}</ref> was a Japanese {{ill|archaeologist|ja|考古学者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/archaeologist|qq=}} specializing in East Asian archaeology, particularly in [[:en:Archaeology of China|Chinese archaeology]]. He served as a professor emeritus at [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]]<ref name=":0" /> and was instrumental in establishing the foundations of East Asian archaeology.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=梅原末治 |url=http://www.toyo-bunko.or.jp/library3/shozou/umehara.html |website=www.toyo-bunko.or.jp |access-date=2022-08-22 |publisher=公益財団法人[[東洋文庫]]}}</ref> He earned his Doctor of Letters (文学博士) from Kyoto Imperial University in 1939 for his dissertation, ''A Study of the Bronze Age in China'' (支那青銅器時代の研究).
== Biography ==
Umehara was born in {{ill|Furuichi Village|ja|古市町 (大阪府)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Village|qq=}}, [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture (now [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]]).<ref name=":1" /> He graduated from Dōshisha Junior High School (now {{ill|Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|ja|同志社中学校・高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|qq=}}) in 1913.<ref name="rireki">[https://kensaku.kua1.archives.kyoto-u.ac.jp/rireki/?c=detail&id=000149 詳細情報一覧/歴代総長・教授・助教授履歴検索システム－対象：1949th year 以前の在職者―]</ref> In 1914, he became an assistant at the Department of Archaeology at Kyoto Imperial University. In 1921, he was a11ointed as a member of the Archaeological Survey Committee of the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government-General of Korea|qq=}}. He joined the {{ill|Kyoto Research Institute|ja|東方文化学院京都研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Research Institute|qq=}} (now the [[:en:The Kyoto University Research Centre for the Cultural Sciences|Institute for Research in Humanities, Kyoto University]]) in 1929 and became a lecturer at Kyoto Imperial University.<ref name=":0" /> In June 1933, he was promoted to associate professor in the Department of Archaeology.<ref name="rireki" /> In 1939, he obtained his doctorate and was a11ointed professor.<ref name="rireki" />
After his retirement in 1956, Umehara was named professor emeritus at Kyoto University.<ref name="rireki" />
== Contributions ==
Despite being frail and unable to pursue higher education initially, Umehara studied under [[:en:Kōsaku Hamada|Kosaku Hamada]], the founder of the archaeology department at Kyoto University. Umehara's archaeological research spanned Japan, Korea, and China, where he contributed significantly to the establishment of typological methods in Japanese archaeology.
His distinctive personality led to conflicts with many contemporary archaeologists, including {{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masao Suenaga|qq=}} and {{ill|Yukio Kobayashi|ja|小林行雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukio Kobayashi|qq=}}.
Umehara's son, {{ill|Kaoru Umehara|ja|梅原郁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaoru Umehara|qq=}}, also became a distinguished archaeologist and professor emeritus at Kyoto University, specializing in East Asian history and winning the Japan Academy Prize in 2010.<ref>{{Cite journal|title=梅原郁先生略歴・主要業績目録 |url=http://id.nii.ac.jp/1642/00000645/ |journal=就実大学史学論集 |volume=36 |pages=9-22 |publisher=[[Shujitsu University]]|date=2022-03}}</ref>
== Awards ==
* 1962: [[:en:Asahi Prize|Asahi Prize]]<ref name=":0" /> * 1963: [[:en:Person of Cultural Merit|Person of Cultural Merit]]<ref name=":0" /> * Order of the Rising Sun, Gold and Silver Star (勲二等旭日重光章)
== Selected Works ==
=== Books ===
* ''Study on Yayoi Pottery Styles'' (1919) * ''Research on Bronze Bells'' (1927) * ''Research on Chinese Ancient Mirrors'' (1931) * ''Collected Research on Chinese Archaeology'' (1938) * ''The Culture of Ancient Korea'' (1946) * ''Archaeological Observations on Ancient East Asia'' (1947) * ''Archaeology over Sixty Years'' (1973)
=== Co-authored and Edited ===
* ''Comprehensive Overview of Ancient Korean Culture'' (1947–1966) * ''The Tombs of Ancient Korea'' (1973)

revision.comment: Bot: remove invalid enwiki interwiki links
revision.text:
'''Sueji Umehara''' (梅原 末治, August 13, 1893 – February 19, 1983)<ref name=":0">{{Citation|title=本会顧問 梅原末治博士訃|author=有光教一|year=1983-07-01|url=https://doi.org/10.14989/shirin_66_591|publisher=史学研究会 (京都大学文学部内)|language=ja|doi=10.14989/shirin_66_591|access-date=2022-08-22}}</ref> was a Japanese {{ill|archaeologist|ja|考古学者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/archaeologist|qq=}} specializing in East Asian archaeology, particularly in [[:en:Archaeology of China|Chinese archaeology]]. He served as a professor emeritus at [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]]<ref name=":0" /> and was instrumental in establishing the foundations of East Asian archaeology.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=梅原末治 |url=http://www.toyo-bunko.or.jp/library3/shozou/umehara.html |website=www.toyo-bunko.or.jp |access-date=2022-08-22 |publisher=公益財団法人[[東洋文庫]]}}</ref> He earned his Doctor of Letters (文学博士) from Kyoto Imperial University in 1939 for his dissertation, ''A Study of the Bronze Age in China'' (支那青銅器時代の研究).
== Biography ==
Umehara was born in {{ill|Furuichi Village|ja|古市町 (大阪府)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Village|qq=}}, [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture (now [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]]).<ref name=":1" /> He graduated from Dōshisha Junior High School (now {{ill|Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|ja|同志社中学校・高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|qq=}}) in 1913.<ref name="rireki">[https://kensaku.kua1.archives.kyoto-u.ac.jp/rireki/?c=detail&id=000149 詳細情報一覧/歴代総長・教授・助教授履歴検索システム－対象：1949th year 以前の在職者―]</ref> In 1914, he became an assistant at the Department of Archaeology at Kyoto Imperial University. In 1921, he was a11ointed as a member of the Archaeological Survey Committee of the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government-General of Korea|qq=}}. He joined the {{ill|Kyoto Research Institute|ja|東方文化学院京都研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Research Institute|qq=}} (now the [[:en:The Kyoto University Research Centre for the Cultural Sciences|Institute for Research in Humanities, Kyoto University]]) in 1929 and became a lecturer at Kyoto Imperial University.<ref name=":0" /> In June 1933, he was promoted to associate professor in the Department of Archaeology.<ref name="rireki" /> In 1939, he obtained his doctorate and was a11ointed professor.<ref name="rireki" />
After his retirement in 1956, Umehara was named professor emeritus at Kyoto University.<ref name="rireki" />
== Contributions ==
Despite being frail and unable to pursue higher education initially, Umehara studied under [[:en:Kōsaku Hamada|Kosaku Hamada]], the founder of the archaeology department at Kyoto University. Umehara's archaeological research spanned Japan, Korea, and China, where he contributed significantly to the establishment of typological methods in Japanese archaeology.
His distinctive personality led to conflicts with many contemporary archaeologists, including {{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masao Suenaga|qq=}} and {{ill|Yukio Kobayashi|ja|小林行雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukio Kobayashi|qq=}}.
Umehara's son, {{ill|Kaoru Umehara|ja|梅原郁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaoru Umehara|qq=}}, also became a distinguished archaeologist and professor emeritus at Kyoto University, specializing in East Asian history and winning the Japan Academy Prize in 2010.<ref>{{Cite journal|title=梅原郁先生略歴・主要業績目録 |url=http://id.nii.ac.jp/1642/00000645/ |journal=就実大学史学論集 |volume=36 |pages=9-22 |publisher=[[Shujitsu University]]|date=2022-03}}</ref>
== Awards ==
* 1962: [[:en:Asahi Prize|Asahi Prize]]<ref name=":0" /> * 1963: [[:en:Person of Cultural Merit|Person of Cultural Merit]]<ref name=":0" /> * Order of the Rising Sun, Gold and Silver Star (勲二等旭日重光章)
== Selected Works ==
=== Books ===
* ''Study on Yayoi Pottery Styles'' (1919) * ''Research on Bronze Bells'' (1927) * ''Research on Chinese Ancient Mirrors'' (1931) * ''Collected Research on Chinese Archaeology'' (1938) * ''The Culture of Ancient Korea'' (1946) * ''Archaeological Observations on Ancient East Asia'' (1947) * ''Archaeology over Sixty Years'' (1973)
=== Co-authored and Edited ===

Umehara's son, {{ill|Kaoru Umehara|ja|梅原郁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaoru Umehara|qq=}}, also became a distinguished archaeologist and professor emeritus at Kyoto University, specializing in East Asian history and winning the Japan Academy Prize in 2010.<ref>{{Cite journal|title=梅原郁先生略歴・主要業績目録 |url=http://id.nii.ac.jp/1642/00000645/ |journal=就実大学史学論集 |volume=36 |pages=9-22 |publisher=[[Shujitsu University]]|date=2022-03}}</ref>
== Awards ==
* 1962: [[:en:Asahi Prize|Asahi Prize]]<ref name=":0" /> * 1963: [[:en:Person of Cultural Merit|Person of Cultural Merit]]<ref name=":0" /> * Order of the Rising Sun, Gold and Silver Star (勲二等旭日重光章)
== Selected Works ==
=== Books ===
* ''Study on Yayoi Pottery Styles'' (1919) * ''Research on Bronze Bells'' (1927) * ''Research on Chinese Ancient Mirrors'' (1931) * ''Collected Research on Chinese Archaeology'' (1938) * ''The Culture of Ancient Korea'' (1946) * ''Archaeological Observations on Ancient East Asia'' (1947) * ''Archaeology over Sixty Years'' (1973)

* ''Comprehensive Overview of Ancient Korean Culture'' (1947–1966) * ''The Tombs of Ancient Korea'' (1973)

revision.comment: Merge [[Category:京都大学人文科学研究所の人物]] into [[Category:People of the Institute for Humanities, Kyoto University]] via bot
revision.text:
'''Sueji Umehara''' (梅原 末治, August 13, 1893 – February 19, 1983)<ref name=":0">{{Citation|title=本会顧問 梅原末治博士訃|author=有光教一|year=1983-07-01|url=https://doi.org/10.14989/shirin_66_591|publisher=史学研究会 (京都大学文学部内)|language=ja|doi=10.14989/shirin_66_591|access-date=2022-08-22}}</ref> was a Japanese {{ill|archaeologist|ja|考古学者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/archaeologist|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 考古学}} specializing in East Asian archaeology, particularly in [[:en:Archaeology of China|Chinese archaeology]]. He served as a professor emeritus at [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]]<ref name=":0" /> and was instrumental in establishing the foundations of East Asian archaeology.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=梅原末治 |url=http://www.toyo-bunko.or.jp/library3/shozou/umehara.html |website=www.toyo-bunko.or.jp |access-date=2022-08-22 |publisher=公益財団法人[[東洋文庫]]}}</ref> He earned his Doctor of Letters (文学博士) from Kyoto Imperial University in 1939 for his dissertation, ''A Study of the Bronze Age in China'' (支那青銅器時代の研究).
== Biography ==
Umehara was born in {{ill|Furuichi Village|ja|古市町 (大阪府)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Village|qq=}}, [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture (now [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]]).<ref name=":1" /> He graduated from Dōshisha Junior High School (now {{ill|Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|ja|同志社中学校・高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|qq=}}) in 1913.<ref name="rireki">[https://kensaku.kua1.archives.kyoto-u.ac.jp/rireki/?c=detail&id=000149 詳細情報一覧/歴代総長・教授・助教授履歴検索システム－対象：1949th year 以前の在職者―]</ref> In 1914, he became an assistant at the Department of Archaeology at Kyoto Imperial University. In 1921, he was a11ointed as a member of the Archaeological Survey Committee of the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government-General of Korea|qq=}}. He joined the {{ill|Kyoto Research Institute|ja|東方文化学院京都研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Research Institute|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now the [[:en:The Kyoto University Research Centre for the Cultural Sciences|Institute for Research in Humanities, Kyoto University]]) in 1929 and became a lecturer at Kyoto Imperial University.<ref name=":0" /> In June 1933, he was promoted to associate professor in the Department of Archaeology.<ref name="rireki" /> In 1939, he obtained his doctorate and was a11ointed professor.<ref name="rireki" />
After his retirement in 1956, Umehara was named professor emeritus at Kyoto University.<ref name="rireki" />
== Contributions ==
Despite being frail and unable to pursue higher education initially, Umehara studied under [[:en:Kōsaku Hamada|Kosaku Hamada]], the founder of the archaeology department at Kyoto University. Umehara's archaeological research spanned Japan, Korea, and China, where he contributed significantly to the establishment of typological methods in Japanese archaeology.
His distinctive personality led to conflicts with many contemporary archaeologists, including {{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masao Suenaga|qq=}} and {{ill|Yukio Kobayashi|ja|小林行雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukio Kobayashi|qq=}}.
Umehara's son, {{ill|Kaoru Umehara|ja|梅原郁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaoru Umehara|qq=}}, also became a distinguished archaeologist and professor emeritus at Kyoto University, specializing in East Asian history and winning the Japan Academy Prize in 2010.<ref>{{Cite journal|title=梅原郁先生略歴・主要業績目録 |url=http://id.nii.ac.jp/1642/00000645/ |journal=就実大学史学論集 |volume=36 |pages=9-22 |publisher=[[Shujitsu University]]|date=2022-03}}</ref>
== Awards ==
* 1962: [[:en:Asahi Prize|Asahi Prize]]<ref name=":0" /> * 1963: [[:en:Person of Cultural Merit|Person of Cultural Merit]]<ref name=":0" /> * Order of the Rising Sun, Gold and Silver Star (勲二等旭日重光章)
== Selected Works ==
=== Books ===
* ''Study on Yayoi Pottery Styles'' (1919) * ''Research on Bronze Bells'' (1927) * ''Research on Chinese Ancient Mirrors'' (1931) * ''Collected Research on Chinese Archaeology'' (1938) * ''The Culture of Ancient Korea'' (1946) * ''Archaeological Observations on Ancient East Asia'' (1947) * ''Archaeology over Sixty Years'' (1973)
=== Co-authored and Edited ===
* ''Comprehensive Overview of Ancient Korean Culture'' (1947–1966) * ''The Tombs of Ancient Korea'' (1973)
== References ==

Umehara was born in {{ill|Furuichi Village|ja|古市町 (大阪府)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Village|qq=}}, [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture (now [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]]).<ref name=":1" /> He graduated from Dōshisha Junior High School (now {{ill|Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|ja|同志社中学校・高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|qq=}}) in 1913.<ref name="rireki">[https://kensaku.kua1.archives.kyoto-u.ac.jp/rireki/?c=detail&id=000149 詳細情報一覧/歴代総長・教授・助教授履歴検索システム－対象：1949th year 以前の在職者―]</ref> In 1914, he became an assistant at the Department of Archaeology at Kyoto Imperial University. In 1921, he was a11ointed as a member of the Archaeological Survey Committee of the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government-General of Korea|qq=}}. He joined the {{ill|Kyoto Research Institute|ja|東方文化学院京都研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Research Institute|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now the [[:en:The Kyoto University Research Centre for the Cultural Sciences|Institute for Research in Humanities, Kyoto University]]) in 1929 and became a lecturer at Kyoto Imperial University.<ref name=":0" /> In June 1933, he was promoted to associate professor in the Department of Archaeology.<ref name="rireki" /> In 1939, he obtained his doctorate and was a11ointed professor.<ref name="rireki" />
After his retirement in 1956, Umehara was named professor emeritus at Kyoto University.<ref name="rireki" />
== Contributions ==
Despite being frail and unable to pursue higher education initially, Umehara studied under [[:en:Kōsaku Hamada|Kosaku Hamada]], the founder of the archaeology department at Kyoto University. Umehara's archaeological research spanned Japan, Korea, and China, where he contributed significantly to the establishment of typological methods in Japanese archaeology.
His distinctive personality led to conflicts with many contemporary archaeologists, including {{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masao Suenaga|qq=}} and {{ill|Yukio Kobayashi|ja|小林行雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukio Kobayashi|qq=}}.
Umehara's son, {{ill|Kaoru Umehara|ja|梅原郁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaoru Umehara|qq=}}, also became a distinguished archaeologist and professor emeritus at Kyoto University, specializing in East Asian history and winning the Japan Academy Prize in 2010.<ref>{{Cite journal|title=梅原郁先生略歴・主要業績目録 |url=http://id.nii.ac.jp/1642/00000645/ |journal=就実大学史学論集 |volume=36 |pages=9-22 |publisher=[[Shujitsu University]]|date=2022-03}}</ref>
== Awards ==
* 1962: [[:en:Asahi Prize|Asahi Prize]]<ref name=":0" /> * 1963: [[:en:Person of Cultural Merit|Person of Cultural Merit]]<ref name=":0" /> * Order of the Rising Sun, Gold and Silver Star (勲二等旭日重光章)
== Selected Works ==
=== Books ===
* ''Study on Yayoi Pottery Styles'' (1919) * ''Research on Bronze Bells'' (1927) * ''Research on Chinese Ancient Mirrors'' (1931) * ''Collected Research on Chinese Archaeology'' (1938) * ''The Culture of Ancient Korea'' (1946) * ''Archaeological Observations on Ancient East Asia'' (1947) * ''Archaeology over Sixty Years'' (1973)
=== Co-authored and Edited ===
* ''Comprehensive Overview of Ancient Korean Culture'' (1947–1966) * ''The Tombs of Ancient Korea'' (1973)
== References ==
<references/>
== External Links ==
* [http://124.33.215.236/umehara2008/ume_query.html Umehara Archaeological Materials Database (Toyo Bunko)] * [http://124.33.215.236/umeharajpnjomon/umejpjmn_open_srchinput.php Japanese Jomon Period Archaeological Materials (Toyo Bunko)]

His distinctive personality led to conflicts with many contemporary archaeologists, including {{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masao Suenaga|qq=|1=Masao Suenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519783}} and {{ill|Yukio Kobayashi|ja|小林行雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukio Kobayashi|qq=|1=Yukio Kobayashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11461875}}.
Umehara's son, {{ill|Kaoru Umehara|ja|梅原郁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaoru Umehara|qq=|1=Kaoru Umehara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538418}}, also became a distinguished archaeologist and professor emeritus at Kyoto University, specializing in East Asian history and winning the Japan Academy Prize in 2010.<ref>{{Cite journal|title=梅原郁先生略歴・主要業績目録 |url=http://id.nii.ac.jp/1642/00000645/ |journal=就実大学史学論集 |volume=36 |pages=9-22 |publisher=[[Shujitsu University]]|date=2022-03}}</ref>
== Awards ==
* 1962: [[:en:Asahi Prize|Asahi Prize]]<ref name=":0" /> * 1963: [[:en:Person of Cultural Merit|Person of Cultural Merit]]<ref name=":0" /> * Order of the Rising Sun, Gold and Silver Star (勲二等旭日重光章)
== Selected Works ==
=== Books ===
* ''Study on Yayoi Pottery Styles'' (1919) * ''Research on Bronze Bells'' (1927) * ''Research on Chinese Ancient Mirrors'' (1931) * ''Collected Research on Chinese Archaeology'' (1938) * ''The Culture of Ancient Korea'' (1946) * ''Archaeological Observations on Ancient East Asia'' (1947) * ''Archaeology over Sixty Years'' (1973)
=== Co-authored and Edited ===
* ''Comprehensive Overview of Ancient Korean Culture'' (1947–1966) * ''The Tombs of Ancient Korea'' (1973)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[az:Sueci Umehara]][[de:Umehara Sueji]][[fr:Sueji Umehara]][[ja:梅原末治]][[ru:Умэхара Суэдзи]][[zh:梅原末治]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Sueji Umehara 1925.jpg|birth_date={{birth date|1893|8|13}}|death_date={{death date|1983|2|18}}|birth_place={{ill|Osaka|qid=Q35765}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|qid=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|qid=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|archaeologist|qid=Q3621491}}; {{ill|art historian|qid=Q1792450}}; {{ill|university teacher|qid=Q1622272}}|awards={{ill|Person of Cultural Merit|qid=Q2545449}}|native_name=梅原末治}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q2478238]] Wikidata name: Sueji Umehara [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sueji Umehara''' (梅原 末治, August 13, 1893 – February 19, 1983)<ref name=":0">{{Citation|title=本会顧問 梅原末治博士訃|author=有光教一|year=1983-07-01|url=https://doi.org/10.14989/shirin_66_591|publisher=史学研究会 (京都大学文学部内)|language=ja|doi=10.14989/shirin_66_591|access-date=2022-08-22}}</ref> was a Japanese {{ill|archaeologist|ja|考古学者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/archaeologist|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 考古学|1=archaeology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23498}} specializing in East Asian archaeology, particularly in [[:en:Archaeology of China|Chinese archaeology]]. He served as a professor emeritus at [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]]<ref name=":0" /> and was instrumental in establishing the foundations of East Asian archaeology.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=梅原末治 |url=http://www.toyo-bunko.or.jp/library3/shozou/umehara.html |website=www.toyo-bunko.or.jp |access-date=2022-08-22 |publisher=公益財団法人[[東洋文庫]]}}</ref> He earned his Doctor of Letters (文学博士) from Kyoto Imperial University in 1939 for his dissertation, ''A Study of the Bronze Age in China'' (支那青銅器時代の研究).
== Biography ==
Umehara was born in {{ill|Furuichi Village|ja|古市町 (大阪府)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Village|qq=|1=Furuichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18700234}}, [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture (now [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]]).<ref name=":1" /> He graduated from Dōshisha Junior High School (now {{ill|Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|ja|同志社中学校・高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|qq=|1=Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11414624}}) in 1913.<ref name="rireki">[https://kensaku.kua1.archives.kyoto-u.ac.jp/rireki/?c=detail&id=000149 詳細情報一覧/歴代総長・教授・助教授履歴検索システム－対象：1949th year 以前の在職者―]</ref> In 1914, he became an assistant at the Department of Archaeology at Kyoto Imperial University. In 1921, he was a11ointed as a member of the Archaeological Survey Committee of the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government-General of Korea|qq=}}. He joined the {{ill|Kyoto Research Institute|ja|東方文化学院京都研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Research Institute|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now the [[:en:The Kyoto University Research Centre for the Cultural Sciences|Institute for Research in Humanities, Kyoto University]]) in 1929 and became a lecturer at Kyoto Imperial University.<ref name=":0" /> In June 1933, he was promoted to associate professor in the Department of Archaeology.<ref name="rireki" /> In 1939, he obtained his doctorate and was a11ointed professor.<ref name="rireki" />
After his retirement in 1956, Umehara was named professor emeritus at Kyoto University.<ref name="rireki" />
== Contributions ==
Despite being frail and unable to pursue higher education initially, Umehara studied under [[:en:Kōsaku Hamada|Kosaku Hamada]], the founder of the archaeology department at Kyoto University. Umehara's archaeological research spanned Japan, Korea, and China, where he contributed significantly to the establishment of typological methods in Japanese archaeology.
His distinctive personality led to conflicts with many contemporary archaeologists, including {{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masao Suenaga|qq=|1=Masao Suenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519783}} and {{ill|Yukio Kobayashi|ja|小林行雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukio Kobayashi|qq=|1=Yukio Kobayashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11461875}}.
Umehara's son, {{ill|Kaoru Umehara|ja|梅原郁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaoru Umehara|qq=|1=Kaoru Umehara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538418}}, also became a distinguished archaeologist and professor emeritus at Kyoto University, specializing in East Asian history and winning the Japan Academy Prize in 2010.<ref>{{Cite journal|title=梅原郁先生略歴・主要業績目録 |url=http://id.nii.ac.jp/1642/00000645/ |journal=就実大学史学論集 |volume=36 |pages=9-22 |publisher=[[Shujitsu University]]|date=2022-03}}</ref>

* ''Comprehensive Overview of Ancient Korean Culture'' (1947–1966) * ''The Tombs of Ancient Korea'' (1973)

<!--Sueji Umehara--><!--Sueji Umehara--> [[Category:Academic staff of Kyoto University]] [[Category:People of the Institute for Humanities, Kyoto University]] [[Category:Recipients of the Order of the Rising Sun, 2nd class]] [[Category:Alumni of Doshisha High School]] [[Category:Births in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:People associated with Nara National Museum]] [[Category:Persons of Cultural Merit]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[az:Sueci Umehara]] [[de:Umehara Sueji]] [[fr:Sueji Umehara]] [[ru:Умэхара Суэдзи]] [[zh:梅原末治]] {{wikidata link|Q2478238}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[az:Sueci Umehara]][[de:Umehara Sueji]][[fr:Sueji Umehara]][[ja:梅原末治]][[ru:Умэхара Суэдзи]][[zh:梅原末治]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Sueji Umehara 1925.jpg|birth_date={{birth date|1893|8|13}}|death_date={{death date|1983|2|18}}|birth_place={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|archaeologist|WD=Q3621491}}; {{ill|art historian|WD=Q1792450}}; {{ill|university teacher|WD=Q1622272}}|awards={{ill|Person of Cultural Merit|WD=Q2545449}}|native_name=梅原末治}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q2478238]] Wikidata name: Sueji Umehara [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sueji Umehara''' (梅原 末治, August 13, 1893 – February 19, 1983)<ref name=":0">{{Citation|title=本会顧問 梅原末治博士訃|author=有光教一|year=1983-07-01|url=https://doi.org/10.14989/shirin_66_591|publisher=史学研究会 (京都大学文学部内)|language=ja|doi=10.14989/shirin_66_591|access-date=2022-08-22}}</ref> was a Japanese {{ill|archaeologist|ja|考古学者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/archaeologist|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 考古学|1=archaeology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23498}} specializing in East Asian archaeology, particularly in [[:en:Archaeology of China|Chinese archaeology]]. He served as a professor emeritus at [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]]<ref name=":0" /> and was instrumental in establishing the foundations of East Asian archaeology.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=梅原末治 |url=http://www.toyo-bunko.or.jp/library3/shozou/umehara.html |website=www.toyo-bunko.or.jp |access-date=2022-08-22 |publisher=公益財団法人[[東洋文庫]]}}</ref> He earned his Doctor of Letters (文学博士) from Kyoto Imperial University in 1939 for his dissertation, ''A Study of the Bronze Age in China'' (支那青銅器時代の研究).
== Biography ==
Umehara was born in {{ill|Furuichi Village|ja|古市町 (大阪府)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Village|1=Furuichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18700234}}, [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture (now [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]]).<ref name=":1" /> He graduated from Dōshisha Junior High School (now {{ill|Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|ja|同志社中学校・高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|1=Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11414624}}) in 1913.<ref name="rireki">[https://kensaku.kua1.archives.kyoto-u.ac.jp/rireki/?c=detail&id=000149 詳細情報一覧/歴代総長・教授・助教授履歴検索システム－対象：1949th year 以前の在職者―]</ref> In 1914, he became an assistant at the Department of Archaeology at Kyoto Imperial University. In 1921, he was a11ointed as a member of the Archaeological Survey Committee of the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government-General of Korea|qq=}}. He joined the {{ill|Kyoto Research Institute|ja|東方文化学院京都研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Research Institute|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now the [[:en:The Kyoto University Research Centre for the Cultural Sciences|Institute for Research in Humanities, Kyoto University]]) in 1929 and became a lecturer at Kyoto Imperial University.<ref name=":0" /> In June 1933, he was promoted to associate professor in the Department of Archaeology.<ref name="rireki" /> In 1939, he obtained his doctorate and was a11ointed professor.<ref name="rireki" />
After his retirement in 1956, Umehara was named professor emeritus at Kyoto University.<ref name="rireki" />
== Contributions ==
Despite being frail and unable to pursue higher education initially, Umehara studied under [[:en:Kōsaku Hamada|Kosaku Hamada]], the founder of the archaeology department at Kyoto University. Umehara's archaeological research spanned Japan, Korea, and China, where he contributed significantly to the establishment of typological methods in Japanese archaeology.
His distinctive personality led to conflicts with many contemporary archaeologists, including {{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masao Suenaga|1=Masao Suenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519783}} and {{ill|Yukio Kobayashi|ja|小林行雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukio Kobayashi|1=Yukio Kobayashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11461875}}.
Umehara's son, {{ill|Kaoru Umehara|ja|梅原郁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaoru Umehara|1=Kaoru Umehara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538418}}, also became a distinguished archaeologist and professor emeritus at Kyoto University, specializing in East Asian history and winning the Japan Academy Prize in 2010.<ref>{{Cite journal|title=梅原郁先生略歴・主要業績目録 |url=http://id.nii.ac.jp/1642/00000645/ |journal=就実大学史学論集 |volume=36 |pages=9-22 |publisher=[[Shujitsu University]]|date=2022-03}}</ref>
== Awards ==
* 1962: [[:en:Asahi Prize|Asahi Prize]]<ref name=":0" /> * 1963: [[:en:Person of Cultural Merit|Person of Cultural Merit]]<ref name=":0" /> * Order of the Rising Sun, Gold and Silver Star (勲二等旭日重光章)
== Selected Works ==
=== Books ===
* ''Study on Yayoi Pottery Styles'' (1919) * ''Research on Bronze Bells'' (1927) * ''Research on Chinese Ancient Mirrors'' (1931) * ''Collected Research on Chinese Archaeology'' (1938) * ''The Culture of Ancient Korea'' (1946) * ''Archaeological Observations on Ancient East Asia'' (1947) * ''Archaeology over Sixty Years'' (1973)
=== Co-authored and Edited ===
* ''Comprehensive Overview of Ancient Korean Culture'' (1947–1966) * ''The Tombs of Ancient Korea'' (1973)
== References ==
<references/>
== External Links ==
* [http://124.33.215.236/umehara2008/ume_query.html Umehara Archaeological Materials Database (Toyo Bunko)] * [http://124.33.215.236/umeharajpnjomon/umejpjmn_open_srchinput.php Japanese Jomon Period Archaeological Materials (Toyo Bunko)]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[az:Sueci Umehara]][[de:Umehara Sueji]][[fr:Sueji Umehara]][[ja:梅原末治]][[ru:Умэхара Суэдзи]][[zh:梅原末治]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Sueji Umehara 1925.jpg|birth_date={{birth date|1893|8|13}}|death_date={{death date|1983|2|18}}|birth_place={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|archaeologist|WD=Q3621491}}; {{ill|art historian|WD=Q1792450}}; {{ill|university teacher|WD=Q1622272}}|awards={{ill|Person of Cultural Merit|WD=Q2545449}}|native_name=梅原末治}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q2478238]] Wikidata name: Sueji Umehara [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sueji Umehara''' (梅原 末治, August 13, 1893 – February 19, 1983)<ref name=":0">{{Citation|title=本会顧問 梅原末治博士訃|author=有光教一|year=1983-07-01|url=https://doi.org/10.14989/shirin_66_591|publisher=史学研究会 (京都大学文学部内)|language=ja|doi=10.14989/shirin_66_591|access-date=2022-08-22}}</ref> was a Japanese {{ill|archaeologist|ja|考古学者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/archaeologist|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 考古学|1=archaeology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23498}} specializing in East Asian archaeology, particularly in [[:en:Archaeology of China|Chinese archaeology]]. He served as a professor emeritus at [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]]<ref name=":0" /> and was instrumental in establishing the foundations of East Asian archaeology.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=梅原末治 |url=http://www.toyo-bunko.or.jp/library3/shozou/umehara.html |website=www.toyo-bunko.or.jp |access-date=2022-08-22 |publisher=公益財団法人[[東洋文庫]]}}</ref> He earned his Doctor of Letters (文学博士) from Kyoto Imperial University in 1939 for his dissertation, ''A Study of the Bronze Age in China'' (支那青銅器時代の研究).
== Biography ==
Umehara was born in {{ill|Furuichi Village|ja|古市町 (大阪府)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Village|1=Furuichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18700234}}, [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture (now [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]]).<ref name=":1" /> He graduated from Dōshisha Junior High School (now {{ill|Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|ja|同志社中学校・高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|1=Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11414624}}) in 1913.<ref name="rireki">[https://kensaku.kua1.archives.kyoto-u.ac.jp/rireki/?c=detail&id=000149 詳細情報一覧/歴代総長・教授・助教授履歴検索システム－対象：1949th year 以前の在職者―]</ref> In 1914, he became an assistant at the Department of Archaeology at Kyoto Imperial University. In 1921, he was a11ointed as a member of the Archaeological Survey Committee of the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government-General of Korea|qq=}}. He joined the {{ill|Kyoto Research Institute|ja|東方文化学院京都研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Research Institute|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now the [[:en:The Kyoto University Research Centre for the Cultural Sciences|Institute for Research in Humanities, Kyoto University]]) in 1929 and became a lecturer at Kyoto Imperial University.<ref name=":0" /> In June 1933, he was promoted to associate professor in the Department of Archaeology.<ref name="rireki" /> In 1939, he obtained his doctorate and was a11ointed professor.<ref name="rireki" />
After his retirement in 1956, Umehara was named professor emeritus at Kyoto University.<ref name="rireki" />
== Contributions ==
Despite being frail and unable to pursue higher education initially, Umehara studied under [[:en:Kōsaku Hamada|Kosaku Hamada]], the founder of the archaeology department at Kyoto University. Umehara's archaeological research spanned Japan, Korea, and China, where he contributed significantly to the establishment of typological methods in Japanese archaeology.
His distinctive personality led to conflicts with many contemporary archaeologists, including {{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masao Suenaga|1=Masao Suenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519783}} and {{ill|Yukio Kobayashi|ja|小林行雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukio Kobayashi|1=Yukio Kobayashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11461875}}.
Umehara's son, {{ill|Kaoru Umehara|ja|梅原郁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaoru Umehara|1=Kaoru Umehara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538418}}, also became a distinguished archaeologist and professor emeritus at Kyoto University, specializing in East Asian history and winning the Japan Academy Prize in 2010.<ref>{{Cite journal|title=梅原郁先生略歴・主要業績目録 |url=http://id.nii.ac.jp/1642/00000645/ |journal=就実大学史学論集 |volume=36 |pages=9-22 |publisher=[[Shujitsu University]]|date=2022-03}}</ref>
== Awards ==
* 1962: [[:en:Asahi Prize|Asahi Prize]]<ref name=":0" /> * 1963: [[:en:Person of Cultural Merit|Person of Cultural Merit]]<ref name=":0" /> * Order of the Rising Sun, Gold and Silver Star (勲二等旭日重光章)
== Selected Works ==
=== Books ===
* ''Study on Yayoi Pottery Styles'' (1919) * ''Research on Bronze Bells'' (1927) * ''Research on Chinese Ancient Mirrors'' (1931) * ''Collected Research on Chinese Archaeology'' (1938) * ''The Culture of Ancient Korea'' (1946) * ''Archaeological Observations on Ancient East Asia'' (1947) * ''Archaeology over Sixty Years'' (1973)
=== Co-authored and Edited ===
* ''Comprehensive Overview of Ancient Korean Culture'' (1947–1966) * ''The Tombs of Ancient Korea'' (1973)
== References ==
<references/>
== External Links ==
* [http://124.33.215.236/umehara2008/ume_query.html Umehara Archaeological Materials Database (Toyo Bunko)] * [http://124.33.215.236/umeharajpnjomon/umejpjmn_open_srchinput.php Japanese Jomon Period Archaeological Materials (Toyo Bunko)]

[[Category:Completion tier 1]]

He served as a professor emeritus at [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]]<ref name=":0" /> and was instrumental in establishing the foundations of East Asian archaeology.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=梅原末治 |url=http://www.toyo-bunko.or.jp/library3/shozou/umehara.html |website=www.toyo-bunko.or.jp |access-date=2022-08-22 |publisher=公益財団法人[[東洋文庫]]}}</ref> He earned his Doctor of Letters (文学博士) from Kyoto Imperial University in 1939 for his dissertation, ''A Study of the Bronze Age in China'' (支那青銅器時代の研究).
== Biography ==
Umehara was born in {{ill|Furuichi Village|ja|古市町 (大阪府)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Village|1=Furuichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18700234}}, [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture (now [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]]).<ref name=":1" /> He graduated from Dōshisha Junior High School (now {{ill|Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|ja|同志社中学校・高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|1=Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11414624}}) in 1913.<ref name="rireki">[https://kensaku.kua1.archives.kyoto-u.ac.jp/rireki/?c=detail&id=000149 詳細情報一覧/歴代総長・教授・助教授履歴検索システム－対象：1949th year 以前の在職者―]</ref> In 1914, he became an assistant at the Department of Archaeology at Kyoto Imperial University. In 1921, he was a11ointed as a member of the Archaeological Survey Committee of the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government-General of Korea|qq=}}. He joined the {{ill|Kyoto Research Institute|ja|東方文化学院京都研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Research Institute|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now the [[:en:The Kyoto University Research Centre for the Cultural Sciences|Institute for Research in Humanities, Kyoto University]]) in 1929 and became a lecturer at Kyoto Imperial University.<ref name=":0" /> In June 1933, he was promoted to associate professor in the Department of Archaeology.<ref name="rireki" /> In 1939, he obtained his doctorate and was a11ointed professor.<ref name="rireki" />
After his retirement in 1956, Umehara was named professor emeritus at Kyoto University.<ref name="rireki" />
== Contributions ==
Despite being frail and unable to pursue higher education initially, Umehara studied under [[:en:Kōsaku Hamada|Kosaku Hamada]], the founder of the archaeology department at Kyoto University. Umehara's archaeological research spanned Japan, Korea, and China, where he contributed significantly to the establishment of typological methods in Japanese archaeology.
His distinctive personality led to conflicts with many contemporary archaeologists, including {{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masao Suenaga|1=Masao Suenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519783}} and {{ill|Yukio Kobayashi|ja|小林行雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukio Kobayashi|1=Yukio Kobayashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11461875}}.
Umehara's son, {{ill|Kaoru Umehara|ja|梅原郁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaoru Umehara|1=Kaoru Umehara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538418}}, also became a distinguished archaeologist and professor emeritus at Kyoto University, specializing in East Asian history and winning the Japan Academy Prize in 2010.<ref>{{Cite journal|title=梅原郁先生略歴・主要業績目録 |url=http://id.nii.ac.jp/1642/00000645/ |journal=就実大学史学論集 |volume=36 |pages=9-22 |publisher=[[Shujitsu University]]|date=2022-03}}</ref>
== Awards ==
* 1962: [[:en:Asahi Prize|Asahi Prize]]<ref name=":0" /> * 1963: [[:en:Person of Cultural Merit|Person of Cultural Merit]]<ref name=":0" /> * Order of the Rising Sun, Gold and Silver Star (勲二等旭日重光章)
== Selected Works ==
=== Books ===
* ''Study on Yayoi Pottery Styles'' (1919) * ''Research on Bronze Bells'' (1927) * ''Research on Chinese Ancient Mirrors'' (1931) * ''Collected Research on Chinese Archaeology'' (1938) * ''The Culture of Ancient Korea'' (1946) * ''Archaeological Observations on Ancient East Asia'' (1947) * ''Archaeology over Sixty Years'' (1973)
=== Co-authored and Edited ===
* ''Comprehensive Overview of Ancient Korean Culture'' (1947–1966) * ''The Tombs of Ancient Korea'' (1973)
== References ==
<references/>
== External Links ==

He served as a professor emeritus at [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]]<ref name=":0" /> and was instrumental in establishing the foundations of East Asian archaeology.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=梅原末治 |url=http://www.toyo-bunko.or.jp/library3/shozou/umehara.html |website=www.toyo-bunko.or.jp |access-date=2022-08-22 |publisher=公益財団法人[[東洋文庫]]}}</ref> He earned his Doctor of Letters (文学博士) from Kyoto Imperial University in 1939 for his dissertation, ''A Study of the Bronze Age in China'' (支那青銅器時代の研究).
== Biography ==
Umehara was born in {{ill|Furuichi Village|ja|古市町 (大阪府)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Village|1=Furuichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18700234}}, [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture (now [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]]).<ref name=":1" /> He graduated from Dōshisha Junior High School (now {{ill|Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|ja|同志社中学校・高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|1=Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11414624}}) in 1913.<ref name="rireki">[https://kensaku.kua1.archives.kyoto-u.ac.jp/rireki/?c=detail&id=000149 詳細情報一覧/歴代総長・教授・助教授履歴検索システム－対象：1949th year 以前の在職者―]</ref> In 1914, he became an assistant at the Department of Archaeology at Kyoto Imperial University. In 1921, he was a11ointed as a member of the Archaeological Survey Committee of the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government-General of Korea|qq=}}. He joined the {{ill|Kyoto Research Institute|ja|東方文化学院京都研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Research Institute|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now the [[:en:The Kyoto University Research Centre for the Cultural Sciences|Institute for Research in Humanities, Kyoto University]]) in 1929 and became a lecturer at Kyoto Imperial University.<ref name=":0" /> In June 1933, he was promoted to associate professor in the Department of Archaeology.<ref name="rireki" /> In 1939, he obtained his doctorate and was a11ointed professor.<ref name="rireki" />
After his retirement in 1956, Umehara was named professor emeritus at Kyoto University.<ref name="rireki" />
== Contributions ==
Despite being frail and unable to pursue higher education initially, Umehara studied under [[:en:Kōsaku Hamada|Kosaku Hamada]], the founder of the archaeology department at Kyoto University. Umehara's archaeological research spanned Japan, Korea, and China, where he contributed significantly to the establishment of typological methods in Japanese archaeology.
His distinctive personality led to conflicts with many contemporary archaeologists, including {{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masao Suenaga|1=Masao Suenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519783}} and {{ill|Yukio Kobayashi|ja|小林行雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukio Kobayashi|1=Yukio Kobayashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11461875}}.
Umehara's son, {{ill|Kaoru Umehara|ja|梅原郁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaoru Umehara|1=Kaoru Umehara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538418}}, also became a distinguished archaeologist and professor emeritus at Kyoto University, specializing in East Asian history and winning the Japan Academy Prize in 2010.<ref>{{Cite journal|title=梅原郁先生略歴・主要業績目録 |url=http://id.nii.ac.jp/1642/00000645/ |journal=就実大学史学論集 |volume=36 |pages=9-22 |publisher=[[Shujitsu University]]|date=2022-03}}</ref>
== Awards ==
* 1962: [[:en:Asahi Prize|Asahi Prize]]<ref name=":0" /> * 1963: [[:en:Person of Cultural Merit|Person of Cultural Merit]]<ref name=":0" /> * Order of the Rising Sun, Gold and Silver Star (勲二等旭日重光章)
== Selected Works ==
=== Books ===
* ''Study on Yayoi Pottery Styles'' (1919) * ''Research on Bronze Bells'' (1927) * ''Research on Chinese Ancient Mirrors'' (1931) * ''Collected Research on Chinese Archaeology'' (1938) * ''The Culture of Ancient Korea'' (1946) * ''Archaeological Observations on Ancient East Asia'' (1947) * ''Archaeology over Sixty Years'' (1973)
=== Co-authored and Edited ===
* ''Comprehensive Overview of Ancient Korean Culture'' (1947–1966) * ''The Tombs of Ancient Korea'' (1973)
== References ==
<references/>
== External Links ==
* [http://124.33.215.236/umehara2008/ume_query.html Umehara Archaeological Materials Database (Toyo Bunko)] * [http://124.33.215.236/umeharajpnjomon/umejpjmn_open_srchinput.php Japanese Jomon Period Archaeological Materials (Toyo Bunko)]
{{translated page|ja|梅原末治|version=103135682|comment=Imported full ja history}}
