revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[az:Sueci Umehara]][[de:Umehara Sueji]][[fr:Sueji Umehara]][[ja:梅原末治]][[ru:Умэхара Суэдзи]][[zh:梅原末治]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Sueji Umehara 1925.jpg|birth_date={{birth date|1893|8|13}}|death_date={{death date|1983|2|18}}|birth_place={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|archaeologist|WD=Q3621491}}; {{ill|art historian|WD=Q1792450}}; {{ill|university teacher|WD=Q1622272}}|awards={{ill|Person of Cultural Merit|WD=Q2545449}}|native_name=梅原末治}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q2478238]] Wikidata name: Sueji Umehara [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sueji Umehara''' (梅原 末治, August 13, 1893 – February 19, 1983)<ref name=":0">{{Citation|title=本会顧問 梅原末治博士訃|author=有光教一|year=1983-07-01|url=https://doi.org/10.14989/shirin_66_591|publisher=史学研究会 (京都大学文学部内)|language=ja|doi=10.14989/shirin_66_591|access-date=2022-08-22}}</ref> was a Japanese {{ill|archaeologist|ja|考古学者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/archaeologist|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 考古学|1=archaeology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23498}} specializing in East Asian archaeology, particularly in [[:en:Archaeology of China|Chinese archaeology]]. He served as a professor emeritus at [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]]<ref name=":0" /> and was instrumental in establishing the foundations of East Asian archaeology.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=梅原末治 |url=http://www.toyo-bunko.or.jp/library3/shozou/umehara.html |website=www.toyo-bunko.or.jp |access-date=2022-08-22 |publisher=公益財団法人[[東洋文庫]]}}</ref> He earned his Doctor of Letters (文学博士) from Kyoto Imperial University in 1939 for his dissertation, ''A Study of the Bronze Age in China'' (支那青銅器時代の研究).
== Biography ==
Umehara was born in {{ill|Furuichi Village|ja|古市町 (大阪府)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Village|1=Furuichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18700234}}, [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture (now [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]]).<ref name=":1" /> He graduated from Dōshisha Junior High School (now {{ill|Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|ja|同志社中学校・高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|1=Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11414624}}) in 1913.<ref name="rireki">[https://kensaku.kua1.archives.kyoto-u.ac.jp/rireki/?c=detail&id=000149 詳細情報一覧/歴代総長・教授・助教授履歴検索システム－対象：1949th year 以前の在職者―]</ref> In 1914, he became an assistant at the Department of Archaeology at Kyoto Imperial University. In 1921, he was a11ointed as a member of the Archaeological Survey Committee of the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government-General of Korea|qq=}}. He joined the {{ill|Kyoto Research Institute|ja|東方文化学院京都研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Research Institute|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now the [[:en:The Kyoto University Research Centre for the Cultural Sciences|Institute for Research in Humanities, Kyoto University]]) in 1929 and became a lecturer at Kyoto Imperial University.<ref name=":0" /> In June 1933, he was promoted to associate professor in the Department of Archaeology.<ref name="rireki" /> In 1939, he obtained his doctorate and was a11ointed professor.<ref name="rireki" />
After his retirement in 1956, Umehara was named professor emeritus at Kyoto University.<ref name="rireki" />
== Contributions ==
Despite being frail and unable to pursue higher education initially, Umehara studied under [[:en:Kōsaku Hamada|Kosaku Hamada]], the founder of the archaeology department at Kyoto University. Umehara's archaeological research spanned Japan, Korea, and China, where he contributed significantly to the establishment of typological methods in Japanese archaeology.
His distinctive personality led to conflicts with many contemporary archaeologists, including {{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masao Suenaga|1=Masao Suenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519783}} and {{ill|Yukio Kobayashi|ja|小林行雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukio Kobayashi|1=Yukio Kobayashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11461875}}.
Umehara's son, {{ill|Kaoru Umehara|ja|梅原郁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaoru Umehara|1=Kaoru Umehara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538418}}, also became a distinguished archaeologist and professor emeritus at Kyoto University, specializing in East Asian history and winning the Japan Academy Prize in 2010.<ref>{{Cite journal|title=梅原郁先生略歴・主要業績目録 |url=http://id.nii.ac.jp/1642/00000645/ |journal=就実大学史学論集 |volume=36 |pages=9-22 |publisher=[[Shujitsu University]]|date=2022-03}}</ref>
== Awards ==
* 1962: [[:en:Asahi Prize|Asahi Prize]]<ref name=":0" /> * 1963: [[:en:Person of Cultural Merit|Person of Cultural Merit]]<ref name=":0" /> * Order of the Rising Sun, Gold and Silver Star (勲二等旭日重光章)
== Selected Works ==
=== Books ===
* ''Study on Yayoi Pottery Styles'' (1919) * ''Research on Bronze Bells'' (1927) * ''Research on Chinese Ancient Mirrors'' (1931) * ''Collected Research on Chinese Archaeology'' (1938) * ''The Culture of Ancient Korea'' (1946) * ''Archaeological Observations on Ancient East Asia'' (1947) * ''Archaeology over Sixty Years'' (1973)
=== Co-authored and Edited ===
* ''Comprehensive Overview of Ancient Korean Culture'' (1947–1966) * ''The Tombs of Ancient Korea'' (1973)
== References ==
<references/>
== External Links ==
* [http://124.33.215.236/umehara2008/ume_query.html Umehara Archaeological Materials Database (Toyo Bunko)] * [http://124.33.215.236/umeharajpnjomon/umejpjmn_open_srchinput.php Japanese Jomon Period Archaeological Materials (Toyo Bunko)]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[az:Sueci Umehara]][[de:Umehara Sueji]][[fr:Sueji Umehara]][[ja:梅原末治]][[ru:Умэхара Суэдзи]][[zh:梅原末治]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Sueji Umehara 1925.jpg|birth_date={{birth date|1893|8|13}}|death_date={{death date|1983|2|18}}|birth_place={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|archaeologist|WD=Q3621491}}; {{ill|art historian|WD=Q1792450}}; {{ill|university teacher|WD=Q1622272}}|awards={{ill|Person of Cultural Merit|WD=Q2545449}}|native_name=梅原末治}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q2478238]] Wikidata name: Sueji Umehara [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sueji Umehara''' (梅原 末治, August 13, 1893 – February 19, 1983)<ref name=":0">{{Citation|title=本会顧問 梅原末治博士訃|author=有光教一|year=1983-07-01|url=https://doi.org/10.14989/shirin_66_591|publisher=史学研究会 (京都大学文学部内)|language=ja|doi=10.14989/shirin_66_591|access-date=2022-08-22}}</ref> was a Japanese {{ill|archaeologist|ja|考古学者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/archaeologist|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 考古学|1=archaeology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23498}} specializing in East Asian archaeology, particularly in [[:en:Archaeology of China|Chinese archaeology]]. He served as a professor emeritus at [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]]<ref name=":0" /> and was instrumental in establishing the foundations of East Asian archaeology.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=梅原末治 |url=http://www.toyo-bunko.or.jp/library3/shozou/umehara.html |website=www.toyo-bunko.or.jp |access-date=2022-08-22 |publisher=公益財団法人[[東洋文庫]]}}</ref> He earned his Doctor of Letters (文学博士) from Kyoto Imperial University in 1939 for his dissertation, ''A Study of the Bronze Age in China'' (支那青銅器時代の研究).
== Biography ==
Umehara was born in {{ill|Furuichi Village|ja|古市町 (大阪府)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Village|1=Furuichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18700234}}, [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture (now [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]]).<ref name=":1" /> He graduated from Dōshisha Junior High School (now {{ill|Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|ja|同志社中学校・高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|1=Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11414624}}) in 1913.<ref name="rireki">[https://kensaku.kua1.archives.kyoto-u.ac.jp/rireki/?c=detail&id=000149 詳細情報一覧/歴代総長・教授・助教授履歴検索システム－対象：1949th year 以前の在職者―]</ref> In 1914, he became an assistant at the Department of Archaeology at Kyoto Imperial University. In 1921, he was a11ointed as a member of the Archaeological Survey Committee of the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government-General of Korea|qq=}}. He joined the {{ill|Kyoto Research Institute|ja|東方文化学院京都研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Research Institute|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now the [[:en:The Kyoto University Research Centre for the Cultural Sciences|Institute for Research in Humanities, Kyoto University]]) in 1929 and became a lecturer at Kyoto Imperial University.<ref name=":0" /> In June 1933, he was promoted to associate professor in the Department of Archaeology.<ref name="rireki" /> In 1939, he obtained his doctorate and was a11ointed professor.<ref name="rireki" />
After his retirement in 1956, Umehara was named professor emeritus at Kyoto University.<ref name="rireki" />
== Contributions ==
Despite being frail and unable to pursue higher education initially, Umehara studied under [[:en:Kōsaku Hamada|Kosaku Hamada]], the founder of the archaeology department at Kyoto University. Umehara's archaeological research spanned Japan, Korea, and China, where he contributed significantly to the establishment of typological methods in Japanese archaeology.
His distinctive personality led to conflicts with many contemporary archaeologists, including {{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masao Suenaga|1=Masao Suenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519783}} and {{ill|Yukio Kobayashi|ja|小林行雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukio Kobayashi|1=Yukio Kobayashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11461875}}.
Umehara's son, {{ill|Kaoru Umehara|ja|梅原郁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaoru Umehara|1=Kaoru Umehara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538418}}, also became a distinguished archaeologist and professor emeritus at Kyoto University, specializing in East Asian history and winning the Japan Academy Prize in 2010.<ref>{{Cite journal|title=梅原郁先生略歴・主要業績目録 |url=http://id.nii.ac.jp/1642/00000645/ |journal=就実大学史学論集 |volume=36 |pages=9-22 |publisher=[[Shujitsu University]]|date=2022-03}}</ref>
== Awards ==
* 1962: [[:en:Asahi Prize|Asahi Prize]]<ref name=":0" /> * 1963: [[:en:Person of Cultural Merit|Person of Cultural Merit]]<ref name=":0" /> * Order of the Rising Sun, Gold and Silver Star (勲二等旭日重光章)
== Selected Works ==
=== Books ===
* ''Study on Yayoi Pottery Styles'' (1919) * ''Research on Bronze Bells'' (1927) * ''Research on Chinese Ancient Mirrors'' (1931) * ''Collected Research on Chinese Archaeology'' (1938) * ''The Culture of Ancient Korea'' (1946) * ''Archaeological Observations on Ancient East Asia'' (1947) * ''Archaeology over Sixty Years'' (1973)
=== Co-authored and Edited ===
* ''Comprehensive Overview of Ancient Korean Culture'' (1947–1966) * ''The Tombs of Ancient Korea'' (1973)
== References ==
<references/>
== External Links ==
* [http://124.33.215.236/umehara2008/ume_query.html Umehara Archaeological Materials Database (Toyo Bunko)] * [http://124.33.215.236/umeharajpnjomon/umejpjmn_open_srchinput.php Japanese Jomon Period Archaeological Materials (Toyo Bunko)]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[az:Sueci Umehara]][[de:Umehara Sueji]][[fr:Sueji Umehara]][[ja:梅原末治]][[ru:Умэхара Суэдзи]][[zh:梅原末治]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[az:Sueci Umehara]][[de:Umehara Sueji]][[fr:Sueji Umehara]][[ja:梅原末治]][[ru:Умэхара Суэдзи]][[zh:梅原末治]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[az:Sueci Umehara]][[de:Umehara Sueji]][[fr:Sueji Umehara]][[ja:梅原末治]][[ru:Умэхара Суэдзи]][[zh:梅原末治]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Sueji Umehara 1925.jpg|birth_date={{birth date|1893|8|13}}|death_date={{death date|1983|2|18}}|birth_place={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|archaeologist|WD=Q3621491}}; {{ill|art historian|WD=Q1792450}}; {{ill|university teacher|WD=Q1622272}}|awards={{ill|Person of Cultural Merit|WD=Q2545449}}|native_name=梅原末治}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q2478238]] Wikidata name: Sueji Umehara [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sueji Umehara''' (梅原 末治, August 13, 1893 – February 19, 1983)<ref name=":0">{{Citation|title=本会顧問 梅原末治博士訃|author=有光教一|year=1983-07-01|url=https://doi.org/10.14989/shirin_66_591|publisher=史学研究会 (京都大学文学部内)|language=ja|doi=10.14989/shirin_66_591|access-date=2022-08-22}}</ref> was a Japanese {{ill|archaeologist|ja|考古学者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/archaeologist|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 考古学|1=archaeology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23498}} specializing in East Asian archaeology, particularly in [[:en:Archaeology of China|Chinese archaeology]]. He served as a professor emeritus at [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]]<ref name=":0" /> and was instrumental in establishing the foundations of East Asian archaeology.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=梅原末治 |url=http://www.toyo-bunko.or.jp/library3/shozou/umehara.html |website=www.toyo-bunko.or.jp |access-date=2022-08-22 |publisher=公益財団法人[[東洋文庫]]}}</ref> He earned his Doctor of Letters (文学博士) from Kyoto Imperial University in 1939 for his dissertation, ''A Study of the Bronze Age in China'' (支那青銅器時代の研究).
== Biography ==
Umehara was born in {{ill|Furuichi Village|ja|古市町 (大阪府)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Village|1=Furuichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18700234}}, [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture (now [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]]).<ref name=":1" /> He graduated from Dōshisha Junior High School (now {{ill|Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|ja|同志社中学校・高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|1=Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11414624}}) in 1913.<ref name="rireki">[https://kensaku.kua1.archives.kyoto-u.ac.jp/rireki/?c=detail&id=000149 詳細情報一覧/歴代総長・教授・助教授履歴検索システム－対象：1949th year 以前の在職者―]</ref> In 1914, he became an assistant at the Department of Archaeology at Kyoto Imperial University. In 1921, he was a11ointed as a member of the Archaeological Survey Committee of the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government-General of Korea|qq=}}. He joined the {{ill|Kyoto Research Institute|ja|東方文化学院京都研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Research Institute|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now the [[:en:The Kyoto University Research Centre for the Cultural Sciences|Institute for Research in Humanities, Kyoto University]]) in 1929 and became a lecturer at Kyoto Imperial University.<ref name=":0" /> In June 1933, he was promoted to associate professor in the Department of Archaeology.<ref name="rireki" /> In 1939, he obtained his doctorate and was a11ointed professor.<ref name="rireki" />
After his retirement in 1956, Umehara was named professor emeritus at Kyoto University.<ref name="rireki" />
== Contributions ==
Despite being frail and unable to pursue higher education initially, Umehara studied under [[:en:Kōsaku Hamada|Kosaku Hamada]], the founder of the archaeology department at Kyoto University. Umehara's archaeological research spanned Japan, Korea, and China, where he contributed significantly to the establishment of typological methods in Japanese archaeology.
His distinctive personality led to conflicts with many contemporary archaeologists, including {{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masao Suenaga|1=Masao Suenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519783}} and {{ill|Yukio Kobayashi|ja|小林行雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukio Kobayashi|1=Yukio Kobayashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11461875}}.
Umehara's son, {{ill|Kaoru Umehara|ja|梅原郁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaoru Umehara|1=Kaoru Umehara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538418}}, also became a distinguished archaeologist and professor emeritus at Kyoto University, specializing in East Asian history and winning the Japan Academy Prize in 2010.<ref>{{Cite journal|title=梅原郁先生略歴・主要業績目録 |url=http://id.nii.ac.jp/1642/00000645/ |journal=就実大学史学論集 |volume=36 |pages=9-22 |publisher=[[Shujitsu University]]|date=2022-03}}</ref>
== Awards ==
* 1962: [[:en:Asahi Prize|Asahi Prize]]<ref name=":0" /> * 1963: [[:en:Person of Cultural Merit|Person of Cultural Merit]]<ref name=":0" /> * Order of the Rising Sun, Gold and Silver Star (勲二等旭日重光章)
== Selected Works ==
=== Books ===
* ''Study on Yayoi Pottery Styles'' (1919) * ''Research on Bronze Bells'' (1927) * ''Research on Chinese Ancient Mirrors'' (1931) * ''Collected Research on Chinese Archaeology'' (1938) * ''The Culture of Ancient Korea'' (1946) * ''Archaeological Observations on Ancient East Asia'' (1947) * ''Archaeology over Sixty Years'' (1973)
=== Co-authored and Edited ===
* ''Comprehensive Overview of Ancient Korean Culture'' (1947–1966) * ''The Tombs of Ancient Korea'' (1973)
== References ==
<references/>
== External Links ==
* [http://124.33.215.236/umehara2008/ume_query.html Umehara Archaeological Materials Database (Toyo Bunko)] * [http://124.33.215.236/umeharajpnjomon/umejpjmn_open_srchinput.php Japanese Jomon Period Archaeological Materials (Toyo Bunko)]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[az:Sueci Umehara]][[de:Umehara Sueji]][[fr:Sueji Umehara]][[ja:梅原末治]][[ru:Умэхара Суэдзи]][[zh:梅原末治]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[az:Sueci Umehara]][[de:Umehara Sueji]][[fr:Sueji Umehara]][[ja:梅原末治]][[ru:Умэхара Суэдзи]][[zh:梅原末治]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[az:Sueci Umehara]][[de:Umehara Sueji]][[fr:Sueji Umehara]][[ja:梅原末治]][[ru:Умэхара Суэдзи]][[zh:梅原末治]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Sueji Umehara 1925.jpg|birth_date={{birth date|1893|8|13}}|death_date={{death date|1983|2|18}}|birth_place={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|archaeologist|WD=Q3621491}}; {{ill|art historian|WD=Q1792450}}; {{ill|university teacher|WD=Q1622272}}|awards={{ill|Person of Cultural Merit|WD=Q2545449}}|native_name=梅原末治}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q2478238]] Wikidata name: Sueji Umehara [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sueji Umehara''' (梅原 末治, August 13, 1893 – February 19, 1983)<ref name=":0">{{Citation|title=本会顧問 梅原末治博士訃|author=有光教一|year=1983-07-01|url=https://doi.org/10.14989/shirin_66_591|publisher=史学研究会 (京都大学文学部内)|language=ja|doi=10.14989/shirin_66_591|access-date=2022-08-22}}</ref> was a Japanese {{ill|archaeologist|ja|考古学者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/archaeologist|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 考古学|1=archaeology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23498}} specializing in East Asian archaeology, particularly in [[:en:Archaeology of China|Chinese archaeology]]. He served as a professor emeritus at [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]]<ref name=":0" /> and was instrumental in establishing the foundations of East Asian archaeology.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=梅原末治 |url=http://www.toyo-bunko.or.jp/library3/shozou/umehara.html |website=www.toyo-bunko.or.jp |access-date=2022-08-22 |publisher=公益財団法人[[東洋文庫]]}}</ref> He earned his Doctor of Letters (文学博士) from Kyoto Imperial University in 1939 for his dissertation, ''A Study of the Bronze Age in China'' (支那青銅器時代の研究).
== Biography ==
Umehara was born in {{ill|Furuichi Village|ja|古市町 (大阪府)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Village|1=Furuichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18700234}}, [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture (now [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]]).<ref name=":1" /> He graduated from Dōshisha Junior High School (now {{ill|Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|ja|同志社中学校・高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|1=Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11414624}}) in 1913.<ref name="rireki">[https://kensaku.kua1.archives.kyoto-u.ac.jp/rireki/?c=detail&id=000149 詳細情報一覧/歴代総長・教授・助教授履歴検索システム－対象：1949th year 以前の在職者―]</ref> In 1914, he became an assistant at the Department of Archaeology at Kyoto Imperial University. In 1921, he was a11ointed as a member of the Archaeological Survey Committee of the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government-General of Korea|qq=}}. He joined the {{ill|Kyoto Research Institute|ja|東方文化学院京都研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Research Institute|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now the [[:en:The Kyoto University Research Centre for the Cultural Sciences|Institute for Research in Humanities, Kyoto University]]) in 1929 and became a lecturer at Kyoto Imperial University.<ref name=":0" /> In June 1933, he was promoted to associate professor in the Department of Archaeology.<ref name="rireki" /> In 1939, he obtained his doctorate and was a11ointed professor.<ref name="rireki" />
After his retirement in 1956, Umehara was named professor emeritus at Kyoto University.<ref name="rireki" />
== Contributions ==
Despite being frail and unable to pursue higher education initially, Umehara studied under [[:en:Kōsaku Hamada|Kosaku Hamada]], the founder of the archaeology department at Kyoto University. Umehara's archaeological research spanned Japan, Korea, and China, where he contributed significantly to the establishment of typological methods in Japanese archaeology.
His distinctive personality led to conflicts with many contemporary archaeologists, including {{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masao Suenaga|1=Masao Suenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519783}} and {{ill|Yukio Kobayashi|ja|小林行雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukio Kobayashi|1=Yukio Kobayashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11461875}}.
Umehara's son, {{ill|Kaoru Umehara|ja|梅原郁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaoru Umehara|1=Kaoru Umehara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538418}}, also became a distinguished archaeologist and professor emeritus at Kyoto University, specializing in East Asian history and winning the Japan Academy Prize in 2010.<ref>{{Cite journal|title=梅原郁先生略歴・主要業績目録 |url=http://id.nii.ac.jp/1642/00000645/ |journal=就実大学史学論集 |volume=36 |pages=9-22 |publisher=[[Shujitsu University]]|date=2022-03}}</ref>
== Awards ==
* 1962: [[:en:Asahi Prize|Asahi Prize]]<ref name=":0" /> * 1963: [[:en:Person of Cultural Merit|Person of Cultural Merit]]<ref name=":0" /> * Order of the Rising Sun, Gold and Silver Star (勲二等旭日重光章)
== Selected Works ==
=== Books ===
* ''Study on Yayoi Pottery Styles'' (1919) * ''Research on Bronze Bells'' (1927) * ''Research on Chinese Ancient Mirrors'' (1931) * ''Collected Research on Chinese Archaeology'' (1938) * ''The Culture of Ancient Korea'' (1946) * ''Archaeological Observations on Ancient East Asia'' (1947) * ''Archaeology over Sixty Years'' (1973)
=== Co-authored and Edited ===
* ''Comprehensive Overview of Ancient Korean Culture'' (1947–1966) * ''The Tombs of Ancient Korea'' (1973)
== References ==
<references/>
== External Links ==

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[az:Sueci Umehara]][[de:Umehara Sueji]][[fr:Sueji Umehara]][[ja:梅原末治]][[ru:Умэхара Суэдзи]][[zh:梅原末治]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[az:Sueci Umehara]][[de:Umehara Sueji]][[fr:Sueji Umehara]][[ja:梅原末治]][[ru:Умэхара Суэдзи]][[zh:梅原末治]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[az:Sueci Umehara]][[de:Umehara Sueji]][[fr:Sueji Umehara]][[ja:梅原末治]][[ru:Умэхара Суэдзи]][[zh:梅原末治]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Sueji Umehara 1925.jpg|birth_date={{birth date|1893|8|13}}|death_date={{death date|1983|2|18}}|birth_place={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|archaeologist|WD=Q3621491}}; {{ill|art historian|WD=Q1792450}}; {{ill|university teacher|WD=Q1622272}}|awards={{ill|Person of Cultural Merit|WD=Q2545449}}|native_name=梅原末治}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q2478238]] Wikidata name: Sueji Umehara [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sueji Umehara''' (梅原 末治, August 13, 1893 – February 19, 1983)<ref name=":0">{{Citation|title=本会顧問 梅原末治博士訃|author=有光教一|year=1983-07-01|url=https://doi.org/10.14989/shirin_66_591|publisher=史学研究会 (京都大学文学部内)|language=ja|doi=10.14989/shirin_66_591|access-date=2022-08-22}}</ref> was a Japanese {{ill|archaeologist|ja|考古学者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/archaeologist|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 考古学|1=archaeology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23498}} specializing in East Asian archaeology, particularly in [[:en:Archaeology of China|Chinese archaeology]]. He served as a professor emeritus at [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]]<ref name=":0" /> and was instrumental in establishing the foundations of East Asian archaeology.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=梅原末治 |url=http://www.toyo-bunko.or.jp/library3/shozou/umehara.html |website=www.toyo-bunko.or.jp |access-date=2022-08-22 |publisher=公益財団法人[[東洋文庫]]}}</ref> He earned his Doctor of Letters (文学博士) from Kyoto Imperial University in 1939 for his dissertation, ''A Study of the Bronze Age in China'' (支那青銅器時代の研究).
== Biography ==
Umehara was born in {{ill|Furuichi Village|ja|古市町 (大阪府)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Village|1=Furuichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18700234}}, [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture (now [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]]).<ref name=":1" /> He graduated from Dōshisha Junior High School (now {{ill|Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|ja|同志社中学校・高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|1=Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11414624}}) in 1913.<ref name="rireki">[https://kensaku.kua1.archives.kyoto-u.ac.jp/rireki/?c=detail&id=000149 詳細情報一覧/歴代総長・教授・助教授履歴検索システム－対象：1949th year 以前の在職者―]</ref> In 1914, he became an assistant at the Department of Archaeology at Kyoto Imperial University. In 1921, he was a11ointed as a member of the Archaeological Survey Committee of the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government-General of Korea|qq=}}. He joined the {{ill|Kyoto Research Institute|ja|東方文化学院京都研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Research Institute|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now the [[:en:The Kyoto University Research Centre for the Cultural Sciences|Institute for Research in Humanities, Kyoto University]]) in 1929 and became a lecturer at Kyoto Imperial University.<ref name=":0" /> In June 1933, he was promoted to associate professor in the Department of Archaeology.<ref name="rireki" /> In 1939, he obtained his doctorate and was a11ointed professor.<ref name="rireki" />
After his retirement in 1956, Umehara was named professor emeritus at Kyoto University.<ref name="rireki" />
== Contributions ==
Despite being frail and unable to pursue higher education initially, Umehara studied under [[:en:Kōsaku Hamada|Kosaku Hamada]], the founder of the archaeology department at Kyoto University. Umehara's archaeological research spanned Japan, Korea, and China, where he contributed significantly to the establishment of typological methods in Japanese archaeology.
His distinctive personality led to conflicts with many contemporary archaeologists, including {{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masao Suenaga|1=Masao Suenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519783}} and {{ill|Yukio Kobayashi|ja|小林行雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukio Kobayashi|1=Yukio Kobayashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11461875}}.
Umehara's son, {{ill|Kaoru Umehara|ja|梅原郁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaoru Umehara|1=Kaoru Umehara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538418}}, also became a distinguished archaeologist and professor emeritus at Kyoto University, specializing in East Asian history and winning the Japan Academy Prize in 2010.<ref>{{Cite journal|title=梅原郁先生略歴・主要業績目録 |url=http://id.nii.ac.jp/1642/00000645/ |journal=就実大学史学論集 |volume=36 |pages=9-22 |publisher=[[Shujitsu University]]|date=2022-03}}</ref>
== Awards ==
* 1962: [[:en:Asahi Prize|Asahi Prize]]<ref name=":0" /> * 1963: [[:en:Person of Cultural Merit|Person of Cultural Merit]]<ref name=":0" /> * Order of the Rising Sun, Gold and Silver Star (勲二等旭日重光章)
== Selected Works ==
=== Books ===
* ''Study on Yayoi Pottery Styles'' (1919) * ''Research on Bronze Bells'' (1927) * ''Research on Chinese Ancient Mirrors'' (1931) * ''Collected Research on Chinese Archaeology'' (1938) * ''The Culture of Ancient Korea'' (1946) * ''Archaeological Observations on Ancient East Asia'' (1947) * ''Archaeology over Sixty Years'' (1973)
=== Co-authored and Edited ===
* ''Comprehensive Overview of Ancient Korean Culture'' (1947–1966) * ''The Tombs of Ancient Korea'' (1973)
== References ==
<references/>
== External Links ==
* [http://124.33.215.236/umehara2008/ume_query.html Umehara Archaeological Materials Database (Toyo Bunko)] * [http://124.33.215.236/umeharajpnjomon/umejpjmn_open_srchinput.php Japanese Jomon Period Archaeological Materials (Toyo Bunko)]

After his retirement in 1956, Umehara was named professor emeritus at Kyoto University.<ref name="rireki" />
== Contributions ==
Despite being frail and unable to pursue higher education initially, Umehara studied under {{ill|Kōsaku Hamada|en|Kōsaku Hamada|WD=Q366007|lt=Kosaku Hamada}}, the founder of the archaeology department at Kyoto University. Umehara's archaeological research spanned Japan, Korea, and China, where he contributed significantly to the establishment of typological methods in Japanese archaeology.
His distinctive personality led to conflicts with many contemporary archaeologists, including {{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masao Suenaga|1=Masao Suenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519783}} and {{ill|Yukio Kobayashi|ja|小林行雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukio Kobayashi|1=Yukio Kobayashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11461875}}.
Umehara's son, {{ill|Kaoru Umehara|ja|梅原郁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaoru Umehara|1=Kaoru Umehara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538418}}, also became a distinguished archaeologist and professor emeritus at Kyoto University, specializing in East Asian history and winning the Japan Academy Prize in 2010.<ref>{{Cite journal|title=梅原郁先生略歴・主要業績目録 |url=http://id.nii.ac.jp/1642/00000645/ |journal=就実大学史学論集 |volume=36 |pages=9-22 |publisher=[[Shujitsu University]]|date=2022-03}}</ref>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[az:Sueci Umehara]][[de:Umehara Sueji]][[fr:Sueji Umehara]][[ja:梅原末治]][[ru:Умэхара Суэдзи]][[zh:梅原末治]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[az:Sueci Umehara]][[de:Umehara Sueji]][[fr:Sueji Umehara]][[ja:梅原末治]][[ru:Умэхара Суэдзи]][[zh:梅原末治]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[az:Sueci Umehara]][[de:Umehara Sueji]][[fr:Sueji Umehara]][[ja:梅原末治]][[ru:Умэхара Суэдзи]][[zh:梅原末治]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Sueji Umehara 1925.jpg|birth_date={{birth date|1893|8|13}}|death_date={{death date|1983|2|18}}|birth_place={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|archaeologist|WD=Q3621491}}; {{ill|art historian|WD=Q1792450}}; {{ill|university teacher|WD=Q1622272}}|awards={{ill|Person of Cultural Merit|WD=Q2545449}}|native_name=梅原末治}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q2478238]] Wikidata name: Sueji Umehara [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sueji Umehara''' (梅原 末治, August 13, 1893 – February 19, 1983)<ref name=":0">{{Citation|title=本会顧問 梅原末治博士訃|author=有光教一|year=1983-07-01|url=https://doi.org/10.14989/shirin_66_591|publisher=史学研究会 (京都大学文学部内)|language=ja|doi=10.14989/shirin_66_591|access-date=2022-08-22}}</ref> was a Japanese {{ill|archaeologist|ja|考古学者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/archaeologist|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 考古学|1=archaeology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23498}} specializing in East Asian archaeology, particularly in {{ill|Archaeology of China|en|Archaeology of China|WD=Q4785492|lt=Chinese archaeology}}. He served as a professor emeritus at {{ill|Kyoto University|en|Kyoto University|WD=Q336264|lt=Kyoto University}}<ref name=":0" /> and was instrumental in establishing the foundations of East Asian archaeology.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=梅原末治 |url=http://www.toyo-bunko.or.jp/library3/shozou/umehara.html |website=www.toyo-bunko.or.jp |access-date=2022-08-22 |publisher=公益財団法人[[東洋文庫]]}}</ref> He earned his Doctor of Letters (文学博士) from Kyoto Imperial University in 1939 for his dissertation, ''A Study of the Bronze Age in China'' (支那青銅器時代の研究).
== Biography ==
Umehara was born in {{ill|Furuichi Village|ja|古市町 (大阪府)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Village|1=Furuichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18700234}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|en|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|WD=Q1347323|lt=Minamikawachi District, Osaka}}, Osaka Prefecture (now {{ill|Habikino|en|Habikino|WD=Q694308|lt=Habikino}}).<ref name=":1" /> He graduated from Dōshisha Junior High School (now {{ill|Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|ja|同志社中学校・高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|1=Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11414624}}) in 1913.<ref name="rireki">[https://kensaku.kua1.archives.kyoto-u.ac.jp/rireki/?c=detail&id=000149 詳細情報一覧/歴代総長・教授・助教授履歴検索システム－対象：1949th year 以前の在職者―]</ref> In 1914, he became an assistant at the Department of Archaeology at Kyoto Imperial University. In 1921, he was a11ointed as a member of the Archaeological Survey Committee of the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government-General of Korea|qq=}}. He joined the {{ill|Kyoto Research Institute|ja|東方文化学院京都研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Research Institute|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now the {{ill|The Kyoto University Research Centre for the Cultural Sciences|en|The Kyoto University Research Centre for the Cultural Sciences|WD=Q7745012|lt=Institute for Research in Humanities, Kyoto University}}) in 1929 and became a lecturer at Kyoto Imperial University.<ref name=":0" /> In June 1933, he was promoted to associate professor in the Department of Archaeology.<ref name="rireki" /> In 1939, he obtained his doctorate and was a11ointed professor.<ref name="rireki" />
After his retirement in 1956, Umehara was named professor emeritus at Kyoto University.<ref name="rireki" />
== Contributions ==
Despite being frail and unable to pursue higher education initially, Umehara studied under {{ill|Kōsaku Hamada|en|Kōsaku Hamada|WD=Q366007|lt=Kosaku Hamada}}, the founder of the archaeology department at Kyoto University. Umehara's archaeological research spanned Japan, Korea, and China, where he contributed significantly to the establishment of typological methods in Japanese archaeology.
His distinctive personality led to conflicts with many contemporary archaeologists, including {{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masao Suenaga|1=Masao Suenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519783}} and {{ill|Yukio Kobayashi|ja|小林行雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukio Kobayashi|1=Yukio Kobayashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11461875}}.
Umehara's son, {{ill|Kaoru Umehara|ja|梅原郁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaoru Umehara|1=Kaoru Umehara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538418}}, also became a distinguished archaeologist and professor emeritus at Kyoto University, specializing in East Asian history and winning the Japan Academy Prize in 2010.<ref>{{Cite journal|title=梅原郁先生略歴・主要業績目録 |url=http://id.nii.ac.jp/1642/00000645/ |journal=就実大学史学論集 |volume=36 |pages=9-22 |publisher=[[Shujitsu University]]|date=2022-03}}</ref>
== Awards ==
* 1962: {{ill|Asahi Prize|en|Asahi Prize|WD=Q720434|lt=Asahi Prize}}<ref name=":0" /> * 1963: {{ill|Person of Cultural Merit|en|Person of Cultural Merit|WD=Q2545449|lt=Person of Cultural Merit}}<ref name=":0" /> * Order of the Rising Sun, Gold and Silver Star (勲二等旭日重光章)
== Selected Works ==
=== Books ===
* ''Study on Yayoi Pottery Styles'' (1919) * ''Research on Bronze Bells'' (1927) * ''Research on Chinese Ancient Mirrors'' (1931) * ''Collected Research on Chinese Archaeology'' (1938) * ''The Culture of Ancient Korea'' (1946) * ''Archaeological Observations on Ancient East Asia'' (1947) * ''Archaeology over Sixty Years'' (1973)
=== Co-authored and Edited ===
* ''Comprehensive Overview of Ancient Korean Culture'' (1947–1966) * ''The Tombs of Ancient Korea'' (1973)
== References ==
<references/>
== External Links ==
* [http://124.33.215.236/umehara2008/ume_query.html Umehara Archaeological Materials Database (Toyo Bunko)] * [http://124.33.215.236/umeharajpnjomon/umejpjmn_open_srchinput.php Japanese Jomon Period Archaeological Materials (Toyo Bunko)]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[az:Sueci Umehara]][[de:Umehara Sueji]][[fr:Sueji Umehara]][[ja:梅原末治]][[ru:Умэхара Суэдзи]][[zh:梅原末治]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[az:Sueci Umehara]][[de:Umehara Sueji]][[fr:Sueji Umehara]][[ja:梅原末治]][[ru:Умэхара Суэдзи]][[zh:梅原末治]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[az:Sueci Umehara]][[de:Umehara Sueji]][[fr:Sueji Umehara]][[ja:梅原末治]][[ru:Умэхара Суэдзи]][[zh:梅原末治]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Sueji Umehara 1925.jpg|birth_date={{birth date|1893|8|13}}|death_date={{death date|1983|2|18}}|birth_place={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|archaeologist|WD=Q3621491}}; {{ill|art historian|WD=Q1792450}}; {{ill|university teacher|WD=Q1622272}}|awards={{ill|Person of Cultural Merit|WD=Q2545449}}|native_name=梅原末治}} '''Sueji Umehara''' (梅原 末治, August 13, 1893 – February 19, 1983)<ref name=":0">{{Citation|title=本会顧問 梅原末治博士訃|author=有光教一|year=1983-07-01|url=https://doi.org/10.14989/shirin_66_591|publisher=史学研究会 (京都大学文学部内)|language=ja|doi=10.14989/shirin_66_591|access-date=2022-08-22}}</ref> was a Japanese {{ill|archaeologist|ja|考古学者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/archaeologist|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 考古学|1=archaeology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23498}} specializing in East Asian archaeology, particularly in {{ill|Archaeology of China|en|Archaeology of China|WD=Q4785492|lt=Chinese archaeology}}. He served as a professor emeritus at {{ill|Kyoto University|en|Kyoto University|WD=Q336264|lt=Kyoto University}}<ref name=":0" /> and was instrumental in establishing the foundations of East Asian archaeology.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=梅原末治 |url=http://www.toyo-bunko.or.jp/library3/shozou/umehara.html |website=www.toyo-bunko.or.jp |access-date=2022-08-22 |publisher=公益財団法人[[東洋文庫]]}}</ref> He earned his Doctor of Letters (文学博士) from Kyoto Imperial University in 1939 for his dissertation, ''A Study of the Bronze Age in China'' (支那青銅器時代の研究).
== Biography ==
Umehara was born in {{ill|Furuichi Village|ja|古市町 (大阪府)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Village|1=Furuichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18700234}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|en|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|WD=Q1347323|lt=Minamikawachi District, Osaka}}, Osaka Prefecture (now {{ill|Habikino|en|Habikino|WD=Q694308|lt=Habikino}}).<ref name=":1" /> He graduated from Dōshisha Junior High School (now {{ill|Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|ja|同志社中学校・高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|1=Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11414624}}) in 1913.<ref name="rireki">[https://kensaku.kua1.archives.kyoto-u.ac.jp/rireki/?c=detail&id=000149 詳細情報一覧/歴代総長・教授・助教授履歴検索システム－対象：1949th year 以前の在職者―]</ref> In 1914, he became an assistant at the Department of Archaeology at Kyoto Imperial University. In 1921, he was a11ointed as a member of the Archaeological Survey Committee of the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government-General of Korea|qq=}}. He joined the {{ill|Kyoto Research Institute|ja|東方文化学院京都研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Research Institute|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now the {{ill|The Kyoto University Research Centre for the Cultural Sciences|en|The Kyoto University Research Centre for the Cultural Sciences|WD=Q7745012|lt=Institute for Research in Humanities, Kyoto University}}) in 1929 and became a lecturer at Kyoto Imperial University.<ref name=":0" /> In June 1933, he was promoted to associate professor in the Department of Archaeology.<ref name="rireki" /> In 1939, he obtained his doctorate and was a11ointed professor.<ref name="rireki" />
After his retirement in 1956, Umehara was named professor emeritus at Kyoto University.<ref name="rireki" />
== Contributions ==
Despite being frail and unable to pursue higher education initially, Umehara studied under {{ill|Kōsaku Hamada|en|Kōsaku Hamada|WD=Q366007|lt=Kosaku Hamada}}, the founder of the archaeology department at Kyoto University. Umehara's archaeological research spanned Japan, Korea, and China, where he contributed significantly to the establishment of typological methods in Japanese archaeology.
His distinctive personality led to conflicts with many contemporary archaeologists, including {{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masao Suenaga|1=Masao Suenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519783}} and {{ill|Yukio Kobayashi|ja|小林行雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukio Kobayashi|1=Yukio Kobayashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11461875}}.
Umehara's son, {{ill|Kaoru Umehara|ja|梅原郁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaoru Umehara|1=Kaoru Umehara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538418}}, also became a distinguished archaeologist and professor emeritus at Kyoto University, specializing in East Asian history and winning the Japan Academy Prize in 2010.<ref>{{Cite journal|title=梅原郁先生略歴・主要業績目録 |url=http://id.nii.ac.jp/1642/00000645/ |journal=就実大学史学論集 |volume=36 |pages=9-22 |publisher=[[Shujitsu University]]|date=2022-03}}</ref>
== Awards ==
* 1962: {{ill|Asahi Prize|en|Asahi Prize|WD=Q720434|lt=Asahi Prize}}<ref name=":0" /> * 1963: {{ill|Person of Cultural Merit|en|Person of Cultural Merit|WD=Q2545449|lt=Person of Cultural Merit}}<ref name=":0" /> * Order of the Rising Sun, Gold and Silver Star (勲二等旭日重光章)
== Selected Works ==
=== Books ===
* ''Study on Yayoi Pottery Styles'' (1919) * ''Research on Bronze Bells'' (1927) * ''Research on Chinese Ancient Mirrors'' (1931) * ''Collected Research on Chinese Archaeology'' (1938) * ''The Culture of Ancient Korea'' (1946) * ''Archaeological Observations on Ancient East Asia'' (1947) * ''Archaeology over Sixty Years'' (1973)
=== Co-authored and Edited ===
* ''Comprehensive Overview of Ancient Korean Culture'' (1947–1966) * ''The Tombs of Ancient Korea'' (1973)
== References ==
<references/>
== External Links ==
* [http://124.33.215.236/umehara2008/ume_query.html Umehara Archaeological Materials Database (Toyo Bunko)] * [http://124.33.215.236/umeharajpnjomon/umejpjmn_open_srchinput.php Japanese Jomon Period Archaeological Materials (Toyo Bunko)]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[az:Sueci Umehara]][[de:Umehara Sueji]][[fr:Sueji Umehara]][[ja:梅原末治]][[ru:Умэхара Суэдзи]][[zh:梅原末治]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[az:Sueci Umehara]][[de:Umehara Sueji]][[fr:Sueji Umehara]][[ja:梅原末治]][[ru:Умэхара Суэдзи]][[zh:梅原末治]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[az:Sueci Umehara]][[de:Umehara Sueji]][[fr:Sueji Umehara]][[ja:梅原末治]][[ru:Умэхара Суэдзи]][[zh:梅原末治]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Sueji Umehara 1925.jpg|birth_date={{birth date|1893|8|13}}|death_date={{death date|1983|2|18}}|birth_place={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712|1=Empire of Japan}}; {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17|1=Japan}}|occupation={{ill|archaeologist|WD=Q3621491|1=Archaeologist}}; {{ill|art historian|WD=Q1792450|1=Art historian}}; {{ill|university teacher|WD=Q1622272|1=university teacher}}|awards={{ill|Person of Cultural Merit|WD=Q2545449|1=Person of Cultural Merit}}|native_name=梅原末治}} '''Sueji Umehara''' (梅原 末治, August 13, 1893 – February 19, 1983)<ref name=":0">{{Citation|title=本会顧問 梅原末治博士訃|author=有光教一|year=1983-07-01|url=https://doi.org/10.14989/shirin_66_591|publisher=史学研究会 (京都大学文学部内)|language=ja|doi=10.14989/shirin_66_591|access-date=2022-08-22}}</ref> was a Japanese {{ill|archaeologist|ja|考古学者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/archaeologist|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 考古学|1=Archaeology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23498}} specializing in East Asian archaeology, particularly in {{ill|Archaeology of China|en|Archaeology of China|WD=Q4785492|lt=Chinese archaeology|1=Archaeology of China}}. He served as a professor emeritus at {{ill|Kyoto University|en|Kyoto University|WD=Q336264|lt=Kyoto University|1=Kyoto University}}<ref name=":0" /> and was instrumental in establishing the foundations of East Asian archaeology.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=梅原末治 |url=http://www.toyo-bunko.or.jp/library3/shozou/umehara.html |website=www.toyo-bunko.or.jp |access-date=2022-08-22 |publisher=公益財団法人[[東洋文庫]]}}</ref> He earned his Doctor of Letters (文学博士) from Kyoto Imperial University in 1939 for his dissertation, ''A Study of the Bronze Age in China'' (支那青銅器時代の研究).
== Biography ==
Umehara was born in {{ill|Furuichi Village|ja|古市町 (大阪府)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Village|1=Furuichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18700234}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|en|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|WD=Q1347323|lt=Minamikawachi District, Osaka|1=Minamikawachi District, Osaka}}, Osaka Prefecture (now {{ill|Habikino|en|Habikino|WD=Q694308|lt=Habikino|1=Habikino}}).<ref name=":1" /> He graduated from Dōshisha Junior High School (now {{ill|Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|ja|同志社中学校・高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doshisha Junior and Senior High School|1=Q11414624|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11414624}}) in 1913.<ref name="rireki">[https://kensaku.kua1.archives.kyoto-u.ac.jp/rireki/?c=detail&id=000149 詳細情報一覧/歴代総長・教授・助教授履歴検索システム－対象：1949th year 以前の在職者―]</ref> In 1914, he became an assistant at the Department of Archaeology at Kyoto Imperial University. In 1921, he was a11ointed as a member of the Archaeological Survey Committee of the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government-General of Korea|qq=}}. He joined the {{ill|Kyoto Research Institute|ja|東方文化学院京都研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Research Institute|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now the {{ill|The Kyoto University Research Centre for the Cultural Sciences|en|The Kyoto University Research Centre for the Cultural Sciences|WD=Q7745012|lt=Institute for Research in Humanities, Kyoto University|1=The Kyoto University Research Centre for the Cultural Sciences}}) in 1929 and became a lecturer at Kyoto Imperial University.<ref name=":0" /> In June 1933, he was promoted to associate professor in the Department of Archaeology.<ref name="rireki" /> In 1939, he obtained his doctorate and was a11ointed professor.<ref name="rireki" />
After his retirement in 1956, Umehara was named professor emeritus at Kyoto University.<ref name="rireki" />
== Contributions ==
Despite being frail and unable to pursue higher education initially, Umehara studied under {{ill|Kōsaku Hamada|en|Kōsaku Hamada|WD=Q366007|lt=Kosaku Hamada|1=Kōsaku Hamada}}, the founder of the archaeology department at Kyoto University. Umehara's archaeological research spanned Japan, Korea, and China, where he contributed significantly to the establishment of typological methods in Japanese archaeology.
His distinctive personality led to conflicts with many contemporary archaeologists, including {{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masao Suenaga|1=Masao Suenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519783}} and {{ill|Yukio Kobayashi|ja|小林行雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukio Kobayashi|1=Kobayashi Yukio|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11461875}}.
Umehara's son, {{ill|Kaoru Umehara|ja|梅原郁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaoru Umehara|1=Kaoru Umehara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538418}}, also became a distinguished archaeologist and professor emeritus at Kyoto University, specializing in East Asian history and winning the Japan Academy Prize in 2010.<ref>{{Cite journal|title=梅原郁先生略歴・主要業績目録 |url=http://id.nii.ac.jp/1642/00000645/ |journal=就実大学史学論集 |volume=36 |pages=9-22 |publisher=[[Shujitsu University]]|date=2022-03}}</ref>
== Awards ==
* 1962: {{ill|Asahi Prize|en|Asahi Prize|WD=Q720434|lt=Asahi Prize|1=Asahi Prize}}<ref name=":0" /> * 1963: {{ill|Person of Cultural Merit|en|Person of Cultural Merit|WD=Q2545449|lt=Person of Cultural Merit|1=Person of Cultural Merit}}<ref name=":0" /> * Order of the Rising Sun, Gold and Silver Star (勲二等旭日重光章)
== Selected Works ==
=== Books ===
* ''Study on Yayoi Pottery Styles'' (1919) * ''Research on Bronze Bells'' (1927) * ''Research on Chinese Ancient Mirrors'' (1931) * ''Collected Research on Chinese Archaeology'' (1938) * ''The Culture of Ancient Korea'' (1946) * ''Archaeological Observations on Ancient East Asia'' (1947) * ''Archaeology over Sixty Years'' (1973)
=== Co-authored and Edited ===
* ''Comprehensive Overview of Ancient Korean Culture'' (1947–1966) * ''The Tombs of Ancient Korea'' (1973)
